programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A successful boxer is found murdered shortly after a bout, so Jack steps into the ring to catch the culprit. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A killer gets out of jail on parole and seeks revenge on Steve Sloan, and it appears anyone close to the detective is also in the line of fire. Medical drama, starring Barry Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is hired by a construction boss to replace his female assistant, who bitterly resents the intrusion

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker is called in to track down two property developers wanted for the murder of a priest. Adventure with the modern-day lawman

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A corpse is discovered in a natural history museum, but the CSI detectives are unable to make an immediate identification because a swarm of flesh-eating beetles has partially devoured it. A six-year-old is later found hiding in the building, and proves to be a key witness. Meanwhile, Flack is called to a building site where a man has been poisoned with insulating foam

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A millionaire former CIA man meets a premature end when someone plants a controlled explosive in his lighter. Over the years he had built up an impressive list of enemies, so finding his killer looks like an uphill task, but as the agents start working through the potential culprits, a recovered computer file leads them to an escort service used by a wanted hitman. However, what they don't bank on is the chief suspect turning up dead

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble seeks out Sister Veronica, a nun who had befriended him two years earlier

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A scandal from a college president-elect's unsavoury past threatens a million-dollar endowment for his institution

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team heads into the Arizona desert to locate an artist who might be able to help solve the murder of fellow agent Jack Patterson - and discovers one of her paintings holds the key to the mystery. Meanwhile, DiNozzo learns he is in line for an inheritance

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker finds himself with a serious challenge when a notorious killer is reported to be in Texas. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose methods are rooted in the Old West

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents are alerted to an armoured-car heist in which five men stole $70million in gold bars and killed two armed guards in the process. When Callen and the team investigate, they encounter the dead bodies of all but one of the robbers along with the entire seized haul - but is their luck too good to be true? Meanwhile, Granger partners up with a reluctant Kensi. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. They soon discover that a complicated murder has taken place and identify a pair of suspects. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The family of a child who finds a gun suspected of being a murder weapon at a naval base comes under suspicion. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of his old friend Kent Morrissey, who mysteriously dropped dead while carrying out an operation. Guest starring Jack Klugman

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The co-pilot of a plane carrying Dr Sloan to a conference is murdered by persons unknown mid-flight, and the remainder of the crew members find themselves struck down by a mystery illness. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team inadvertently crashes a bank robbery in progress and soon find themselves taken hostage. Meanwhile, Sylvester's feelings for Florence grow

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. While Gibbs tries to determine the cause, Abby and Jimmy partner with the Naval Medical Research Centre in the hope of delivering a cure before the holidays. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

With a cameo appearance by DJ Scott Mills

Realising how unstable the man is, Smithy mounts a frantic search to stop him reaching the woman's daughter, and catches up with him at her school. As Gina evacuates the building, Smithy takes his life in his hands to disarm the ranting maniac

programme.desc: Huw and Lenny's tenancy is left in question. Nigel helps Dot come to terms with her past. Bianca tries to make Tiffany face the dangers of her game. Mark and Ruth make plans

programme.desc: It's the day of Courtney's Christening. Naturally, Tony's not overjoyed. Nigel is so busy decorating Dot's flat, that he almost forgets the Christening

Drama, guest starring George Cole, Roy Hudd and Jan Francis

programme.desc: David and Anna's plans for a romantic weekend away are shattered when their family and friends decide to join them, and Luke has an important question for Megan. programme.title: Where the Heart Is

programme.desc: The death of Sharon and Tracey's Aunt Sylvie soon has them jetting off to Hollywood in search of their real father. Festive special of the comedy from 1993, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson, with guest appearances by George Hamilton, George Wendt and Richard Branson

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part one of two. The green-fingered amateur sleuths are drawn into another mystery when a barrister employing them to spruce up his garden discovers his cousin has been drowned, only for the man to be spotted alive and well a few days later. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part two of two. The amateur sleuths continue their investigation after Martin Frazer's cousin, who supposedly drowned, is spotted alive and well a few days later. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The annual wine show is a major event in Brokenwood and no one is looking forward to it more than DI Mike, the recent buyer of a 10-acre vineyard. However, when a guest judge and wine critic are found dead, drowned inside a fermenting vat, the show takes second place to the murder investigation. Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson star

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives investigating criminal acts in Stratford-upon-Avon

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: An asylum seeker is murdered on a notorious Edinburgh housing estate in what appears to be a race-hate crime - but Rebus suspects there is something more complex behind it. A local MP also reports a missing Kosovan girl, who was last seen in a car with a known criminal - and it turns out he has vanished as well. Ken Stott stars as Ian Rankin's jaded detective, with Claire Price and Cora Bissett

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Three teenagers are shot dead while enjoying a picnic in a secluded wood. When a colleague of Wallander's is also murdered, he becomes convinced the two crimes are related, and sets out to catch the killer. However, the detective is faced with a lack of clues and an assassin who always seems to be one step ahead. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh and Sarah Smart

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

Elsewhere, an NHS manager dreams up a scam to siphon over £800,000 of NHS money into his own account. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, inviting the questions of a virtual audience from around the UK. On her panel are Secretary of State for Transport Grant Shapps MP, Shadow Secretary for International Trade Emily Thornberry MP, Executive Chairman of the National Care Association Nadra Ahmed, poet, novelist and playwright Kate Clanchy, and broadcaster and journalist Tom Newton Dunn

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned in St Margaret's at Cliffe, Kent, Dalton-in-Furness, Cumbria and Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley follows the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, featuring the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A woman gives birth on the M5 with the help of her husband and an emergency call handler, while a family's car is hit by an oak tree during a storm

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where two teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction on a budget of £300 each. Natasha monitors their progress and also takes a historic look at the semi-precious gemstone Blue John

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Olivia, AF, Gavin and Zoe vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: In St Albans, Hertfordshire, Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. The buyers have up to £800 to spend on something to celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary, but cannot agree on what this should be. Can a sculptor, a ceramicist or a painter resolve their dilemma?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After an epic tour of the south and south west, Raj Bisram and Paul Martin's shopping and sale-room the duo hope to acquire anything 'going for a sonnet'. Their shopping and sale-room extravaganza concludes at a Palladian mansion in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Host Sue Barker and team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell bid farewell to the sports quiz as they are joined by British tennis great Tim Henman, former Aston Villa striker Dion Dublin, Ashes-winning England cricket captain Michael Vaughan and ex-Wales rugby union forward Scott Quinnell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat enjoys making Suki squirm about his dalliance with Sharon and as he dashes out, she gets a call from Jags. Sonia tells Tiffany she is taking it step by step with her dad, while Bobby and Iqra are both battling with their feelings, and Iqra has doubts as to whether Mila really cares

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedy actor David Mitchell hosts the satirical quiz, with guest panellists including comedian Jane Godley joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: The broadcaster takes to the skies with a flight in a spitfire, while This Time co-presenter Jennie comes face to face with her online troll. Plus, new band Cayman perform live on their show. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Bryn Williams and Liv Burt, plus guest Michael Ball. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom maisonette in London, a house in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and a mid-terrace in Goldenhill, Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team hunt for bargains at Exeter's Westpoint Arena. Helping the red and blue teams spend £300 on three items are experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps an Oxford couple find a new home on the West Sussex coast, and dons protective gear to join a conservation project protecting nature's most productive workers

programme.title: Missing Link (2019)
programme.desc: Fearless explorer Sir Lionel Frost is approached by fur-covered, eight-foot-tall creature Mr Link who, tired of living a solitary existence in the Pacific Northwest, wants to find his long-lost relatives in the fabled valley of Shangri-La. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Zach Galifianakis, Zoe Saldana, David Walliams and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ricky Wilson from the Kaiser Chiefs, as they assist a paralympic athlete and her boyfriend from Sheffield in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a chef, a life coach, and a salesman

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Special edition featuring Mike Nolan, Toyah Willcox, Dr Linda Papadopoulos, Dr Zoe Williams, Nathan Caton, Suzi Ruffell, Anthony Costa and Shaun Williamson

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty heads to the clinic seeking revenge for his mother's poor treatment, accompanied by Jan posing as a potential patient - but he receives a distressing gets a call from Jade that changes everything - Bibi has been rushed into the ED after an overdose. Tina is concerned that something is not right with Jacob, who confesses his true feelings for her after a stressful day overwhelms him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. Fenisha's difficult situation is made even more complicated when her former fiance starts working at Holby as a locum registrar. Rash puts his medical training to the test, and Marty channels his anger into a good cause after his mother's suicide attempt

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend 2021
programme.desc: Greg James introduces highlights from this year's virtual festival, including performances by Ed Sheeran, AJ Tracey and Royal Blood recorded in front of iconic backdrops around the UK. The programme also includes highlights of a special show at the BBC Radio Theatre, featuring Celeste, Mabel, Anne-Marie, Wolf Alice and London Grammar

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the greatest Premier League champions

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah visits Scotland, making her way to Dumfries & Galloway with a married couple from London who are seeking a life-changing move and have a £350,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Swindon to meet Saima and her children Hamzah, Hamid and Zakir, who are rather embarrassed by their concrete jungle of a garden. Saima wants a zoned garden that gives her an adult area to entertain as well as a chillout space for the youngsters and has a £2,500 budget for the conversion

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the last four contestants have to make a cuff bracelet, then attempt to create an engagement ring for couple James and Clare, before judges Shaun Leane and Solange Azagury-Partridge decide who to put through to the final

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin teaches two would-be antiques dealers the tricks of the trade, pitching David Stead from Derbyshire against Laura Gray from Chichester, before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West heats continue with the fish course, and Tom Brown, Head Chef at Outlaw's at The Capital in Knightsbridge, is hoping he'll be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet. Meanwhile, his competitors, Andy Clatworthy and Dominic Chapman, are looking to score highly with their more luxurious dishes

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West chefs Dominic Chapman, Tom Brown and Andy Clatworthy go all out to impress with their main courses, as they compete for a chance to cook at the Taste of Summer banquet. With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon-related recipe, who will come out on top?

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Gillian Burke follows the fortunes of a harbour seal in hospital in Northern Ireland, while Iolo Williams is on the look out for golden eagles in the Glen. Elsewhere, Michaela Strachan and Chris Packham assess the effects of this year's late spring on our wildlife

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: The wildlife of the mountains, finding out about ravens, the golden eagles of the Scottish hills and asking whether the UK will ever again play host to a long-lost beast - the lynx

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and David Harper assess items brought to Allerton Castle in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin finds out about Harrogate's connection with Russia's last tsarina

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael tracks the River Thames from east to west. Starting in Dagenham, he finishes this leg of his tour at one of London's most iconic buildings, Battersea Power Station, built during the 1930s by Giles Gilbert Scott

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan reveal how Birmingham's Victorian working class residents made their home city one of the most important industrial centres on the planet. They examine the surviving remains of one of the world's oldest railway terminuses, and discover how many of the thousands of skeletons in the cemetery have evidence of bone damage or amputation, as well as signs of the after-effects of industrial pollution. Plus, a look at the true story behind Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring the latest dramas from the live nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to Loch Fleet on Scotland's east coast, hoping to catch ospreys and otters out fishing. Gillian Burke checks out the batty nightlife at WWT Castle Espie, and Megan McCubbin joins conservationists shaping the UK of tomorrow

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis look back on the last seven days in the company of guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Robin Morgan and Ahir Shah

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: DJ Iain Lee gets a call on his phone-in show from Mandy, who tells him how she became trapped in a coffin, having been forced into a robbery. Comedy short written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Natalie Cassidy

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emma Barnett

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: Desperate for success and tempted by the lucrative rewards brought by towing the line, Jimmy White joined Barry Hearn in the hope he could be turned into a champion like his new stablemate Steve Davis. With White as the hot-favourite and with the crowd urging him on against 21-year-old Stephen Hendry, the final of 1990 world championship turned out to be a pivotal moment in British snooker and paved the way for what was to come in the next decade

programme.title: Makeup: A Glamorous History
programme.desc: Makeup artist Lisa Eldridge explores what the beauty looks of three iconic moments in British history reveal about that era, continuing with the Victorians. In Victorian Britain, makeup was frowned upon, and women were supposed to be naturally beautiful, reflecting their inner purity. Throughout the programme, Lisa delves into this obsession with natural beauty and learns about some of the products created to help achieve this, before creating her own 'no makeup' look inspired by Victorian beauty

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's children's week in the sewing room and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young's pattern challenge choice is a classic for any toddler's wardrobe - a romper suit. The transformation task sees the sewers turning an adult wetsuit into a kids' fancy dress costume, before they tackle a children's raincoat, remembering it must be fully waterproof but allow enough movement for jumping in muddy puddles

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits Staffordshire to investigate a damp and dingy two-bed terrace, before heading to Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where he discovers a neglected detached house with some beautiful period features. Meanwhile, Martel Maxwell goes to Hertfordshire to find out just what can be done with a pair of old garages

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Will Kirk brings his expertise to bear on a carved walnut piano stool which has met with an untimely accident, leaving it with only three legs. Horologist Steve Fletcher's knowledge of dials, numbers and needles is called upon to resurrect a broken speedometer from 1920s motor car, and basket-maker Sarah Hatton works on a wicker baby crib that has fallen into disrepair. Finally, Brenton West and Will join forces to restore a 19th-century tippling stick - a walking stick with a hidden tube for whisky inside - thought to have once belonged to King George V

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Contestants from Essex and Shropshire take part in the antiques challenge, presented by Paul Martin. The pair are shown the tricks of the trade before an auction showdown

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Tom Brown, Andy Clatworthy and returning competitor Dominic Chapman continue the battle to cook at a Taste of Summer banquet celebrating 140 years of the Wimbledon Championships. As each of them prepare their desserts, they will all be out to impress as only two can make it through to tomorrow's South West final

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining South West chefs compete for a place in the national finals, preparing their entire menus for judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Marion Regan. Marion's farm provides the famous Wimbledon strawberries to the Championships, so her involvement is entirely suitable given the ongoing tennis theme to the series

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan bring the latest dramas from the live nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to Loch Fleet on Scotland's east coast, hoping to catch ospreys and otters out fishing. Gillian Burke checks out the batty nightlife at WWT Castle Espie, and Megan McCubbin joins conservationists shaping the UK of tomorrow

programme.title: Wild UK
programme.desc: Colin Stafford-Johnson and Lucy Cooke examine wildlife in cities. Colin travels to Newcastle to meet up with a seabird with a liking for high-rise living, while Lucy heads to Brighton to meet Dr Dawn Scott, who is unravelling the reasons behind the red fox's urban habits. Plus, how life for the people of Alaska can be governed by the arrival of the Pacific salmon

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat Safari Park once again, meeting the osteopath helping to treat the arthritis of a rescued circus elephant

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Coverage of the second meeting of the season, which comes from Khalifa International Stadium in Doha, Qatar. Some of the world's leading sprinters will be in action in the men's 200m and 400m and women's 400m, while home favourites Mutaz Barshim and Abderrahman Samba are set to take part in the high jump and 400m hurdles respectively

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: How age-blind casting, such as Ian McKellen in a new production of Hamlet, can change the way we perceive creative work. Lionel Shriver, whose new novel is about a married medical couple contemplating ending their lives in old age, discusses why her characters change their minds as they get older. And Mary Beard finds out why former Coronation Street star Julie Hesmondhalgh has written a play she can perform for the rest of her life

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tries to spot one of the most photogenic birds in the UK - the black-throated diver. Plus, live updates from Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan in Norfolk - is there any sign of the elusive turtle dove?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives advice on plants to use for foliage with tropical proportions. Plus, Arit Anderson continues investigating the use of peat, and Carol Klein explores Oxford Botanic Garden's euphorbias

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The pianist welcomes Dublin-born singer songwriter Imelda May, who discusses her new album, 11 Past the Hour. Jools also welcomes British-Nigerian musician and actor Little Simz, and Grantham-born rising star Holly Humberstone. Plus, Sigrid performs a track from her forthcoming second album

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Effie Gray (2014)
programme.desc: A Victorian woman is trapped in a loveless marriage to a celebrated art critic from the age of 19. She becomes a model for a painter her husband is promoting and falls in love with him. Inspired by the relationship and aided by a respected writer, she creates scandal by becoming the first woman to seek a divorce. Fact-based period drama, starring Dakota Fanning and Emma Thompson

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: An uprising ensues as Tom Zarek's plan to oust the Admiral gains momentum when crew members join forces with civilian rebels - leaving Adama at the mercy of the Cylons who offer to protect him and the President from the mutinous band. Edward James Olmos, Mary McDonnell and Katee Sackhoff star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The uprising continues and leads to Tom Zarek staging a military coup. The president is in hiding on the Cylon ship, and Apollo, Starbuck and the Cylons must get to Bill Adama before he is executed and regain control of the vessel

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Navy veteran David, who survived an attack on the SS Atlantic Conveyor in May 1982, during the Falklands war. David is desperate to reconnect with his fellow crewmen. Plus, the story of Darryl, an author whose family came to the UK as part of the post-war wave of migration from former British colonies. He searches for the teachers who helped him overcome childhood racism and xenophobia. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his travels through England's Home Counties at Stoke Mandeville in Buckinghamshire, where he discovers the legacy of a German Jewish doctor who fled the Nazis to settle in Britain. He then heads through the Chiltern Hills to Princes Risborough, before reaching Beaconsfield and revisiting his much-loved childhood haunt, Bekonscot Model Village

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce a collection of weird clips from the natural world, featuring a solar death ray that's frying birds mid-flight, how the sun changed the course of musical history with a violin worth 15 million dollars, and a shocking new discovery - spider goats

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Amelie wants a Queen Victoria den, where she can pass on her love of history to her friends

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Tara and Caleb battle it out, and are joined by comedians Jarred Christmas, Ola Labib and Nathan Caton, and CBBC's Naomi Wilkinson

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: From the Martin Mere Wetland Centre

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Highlights of the series in which Steve Backshall undertook a global quest from the Arctic Circle to Antarctica, encountering the world's deadliest animals in their natural habitats. Featuring the presenter's encounters with polar bears and leopard seals, his failed attempts to stand up to tornado-force winds and scorching-hot lava flows, and previously unseen footage of his bumps, trips and slips

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: In episode two, Professor Brian Cox is off to India, where he assesses arguably the first evidence of rational thought in literature, the poetry of the Vedic monks. They pondered mankind's origins, realising there must have been a day with no yesterday - a day of creation - prompting the age-old question of where did the universe come from? Brian marvels that the universe seems to follow a set of rules, the laws of physics, allowing space to be considered on the grandest scale, travelling to the most distant, farthest reaches of the cosmos just by using our minds. Brian also visits Japan, and offers viewers the idea that man lives in just one of an infinite number of universes that are being made all the time

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Ageing silverback leader Titus faces his greatest challenge when he tries to care for a vulnerable orphan, while hot-headed youngster Marembo sets about making his presence felt in the mountains of Uganda. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Tycoon (1947)
programme.desc: A civil engineer assigned to build a railroad through the South American Andes causes friction when he refuses to work on a proposed tunnel through the mountains. He eventually gets his own way, and it looks as if he and his arrogant employer will settle their differences - until the engineer falls for his boss's daughter. Romantic drama, starring John Wayne, Cedric Hardwicke, Laraine Day and Anthony Quinn

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A group of sea anglers from Zimbabwe chance their hand in the rolling seas off the coast of Yorkshire

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tries to spot one of the most photogenic birds in the UK - the black-throated diver. Plus, live updates from Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan in Norfolk - is there any sign of the elusive turtle dove?

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon returns with the property makeover show in which cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition architects Laura Jane Clark and Julian McIntosh compete to remodel Shelley and Steven's three-bedroom semi-detached home in Bromley, south-east London

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: Film-maker Rankin sets a series of challenges for six talented amateur photographers. In the first round, they are tested on the speed of their reactions on Brighton seafront, where they must capture stunning images using just a smart phone. They are then joined by Chris Packham in the New Forest to take shots of wildlife, and finally take a celebrity portrait of Anna Friel

programme.title: The Battle of the River Plate (1956)
programme.desc: British naval forces pursue German battleship the Admiral Graf Spee, finally blockading the vessel in Montevideo harbour, where its commanding officer is faced with an impossible choice. Second World War adventure from Powell and Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Anthony Quayle, Peter Finch, Bernard Lee and Jack Gwillim

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo discovers the origins of Harry Beck's map of the London Underground, learns about a shocking surrealist show in 1930s Piccadilly and explores the headquarters of Churchill's war cabinet

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The men head off for a weekend at a training camp, but arrive late and hungry after Mainwaring's map-reading skills are found to be somewhat lacking. They then have to capture an enemy's camp during an exercise, giving the captain a chance to earn back his men's respect. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1984
programme.desc: Contributors including Howard Jones, Nik Kershaw, Paul Young, Nigel Planer and Midge Ure recall the year of big hair and big tunes, when a BBC ban on Frankie Goes to Hollywood's Relax led to an embarrassing gap on the show when it reached the top of the chart. The year saw the rise of one-man acts such as Nik Kershaw and Howard Jones, while Sade brought a stripped-back soulful vibe to jazz pop. German singer Nena hit the top spot with 99 Red Balloons, while the Special AKA's Free Nelson Mandela combined a political message with an irresistible tune. The year ended with the stars of the day pooling their talents with Band Aid's charity single Do They Know It's Christmas?

programme.title: Cher at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes, including Believe, Love and Understanding, and Walking in Memphis, as well as her first UK chart-topper I Got You Babe, recorded with ex-husband and singing partner Sonny Bono

programme.title: Moonstruck (1987)
programme.desc: An Italian-American widow agrees to marry an older man - only to fall head over heels in love with his younger brother, leaving her facing an impossible choice. Romantic comedy, starring Cher in an Oscar-winning role, with Nicolas Cage, Olympia Dukakis and Danny Aiello

programme.title: 20th Century Women (2016)
programme.desc: Dorothea Fields is a determined single mother in her mid-fifties raising her teenage son Jamie in California in 1979. At a time brimming with cultural change and rebellion, she enlists the help of two younger women to help with her son's upbringing. Comedy drama, starring Annette Bening, Elle Fanning, Greta Gerwig, Billy Crudup and Lucas Jade Zumann

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1984
programme.desc: A compilation of performances, featuring the Smiths, Duran Duran, Sade, the Weather Girls, Wham! and Bronski Beat. Further stellar appearances come from the show's debuts of iconic Americans Madonna, Miami Sound Machine and Cyndi Lauper - who runs riot in the studio. Frankie Goes to Hollywood celebrate their chart dominance of the year with one of their celebrated renditions of Two Tribes, while a less credible but arguably more memorable ditty comes courtesy of Black Lace

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the unusually high numbers of wild parakeets in south-east England. Estimates indicate there are more than 30,000 of the birds in the UK but experts have not been able to reach a consensus on how the creatures managed to arrive in the country

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adventurer Paul Rose concludes his exploration of the 79-mile Yorkshire Wolds Way, taking to the skies to get a unique view of its stretch of chalk downland. He also receives a special invitation to a military base that has been keeping the nation safe since the start of the Second World War

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: The accomplished artist depicts a scene with a little cabin and shed, nestled in snowdrifts

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A case of foot-and-mouth brings tragedy to the Dales, leaving Siegfried in quarantine and James and Tristan having to deal with all of the rounds. James discovers the power of anesthesia in assisting animals to heal - first with a farmer's dying ewe and then with the Flaxtons' pet poodle

programme.title: Masked and Anonymous (2003)
programme.desc: Crooked publicists come up with a guaranteed money-maker as they set out to spring an ageing rock star (Bob Dylan, who also co-wrote the script) from prison so he can headline a charity concert. But a canny journalist cottons on to the cynicism driving their scheme and soon looks set to thwart their plans. John Goodman and Jessica Lange co-star in this satire

programme.title: Tangled Up with Dylan: The Ballad of AJ Weberman
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the life of notorious Bob Dylan enthusiast AJ Weberman, who tried to gain access to the singer-songwriter's psyche by analysing his rubbish. Featuring a recorded phone conversation between the duo from the 1970s and testimonies from Weberman's friends, the programme provides an insight into how far fans are willing to go to know more about their heroes

programme.title: Me, My Selfie and I with Ryan Gander
programme.desc: Cconceptual artist Ryan Gander investigates the selfie - the icon of a new kind of self-regard that hardly existed just 10 years ago. He discovers the roots of the selfie go back hundreds of years before smartphones. In the age of social media, he investigates what that really means and what technology is doing to people's sense of self

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford travels to the Hague and Amsterdam to find out what inspired artists such as Rembrandt and Ruysch to create anatomical paintings in the 17th century, and learns how dissecting bodies became considered a suitable subject for art in the period

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: The accomplished artist depicts a scene with a little cabin and shed, nestled in snowdrifts

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adventurer Paul Rose concludes his exploration of the 79-mile Yorkshire Wolds Way, taking to the skies to get a unique view of its stretch of chalk downland. He also receives a special invitation to a military base that has been keeping the nation safe since the start of the Second World War

programme.title: Extra Life: A Short History of Living Longer
programme.desc: Steven Johnson and David Olusoga explore the remarkable and often unsung work that has led to major medical advances. Alexander Fleming's famous discovery of antibiotics was followed - in the midst of the Second World War - by the rollout of the manufacture of penicillin at scale, so that lives might be saved at a global level. Pioneering rational drug design in the 1980s led to the development of antiviral drugs for HIV and other previously untreatable diseases. The knowledge of how to produce safe, effective drugs now underpins the work to find treatments for new diseases, such as Covid-19

programme.title: Pop Go the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the BBC's extensive archives, featuring the long-standing pop group the Kinks, whose success in America followed in the wake of the Beatles and the Rolling Stones

programme.title: Sings Dylan 2
programme.desc: Cover versions of Bob Dylan songs from the 1960s to the present, sourced from the BBC archives. Featuring classic tracks old and new, plus a few surprises. Performers include Joan Baez, Siouxsie and the Banshees, the Hollies, Adele, Julie Felix, Richie Havens, Bryan Ferry and KT Tunstall

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first aired on January 19, featuring performances by Soho, Alexander O'Neal, Seal, Belinda Carlisle, Bill Medley and Jennifer Warnes, the KLF, Sting, Seal, Enigma and Ralph Tresvant

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1991
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the edition first aired on January 24, featuring performances by Tongue 'N' Cheek, Robert Palmer, 2 in a Room, the Simpsons, Off-Shore, a Tribe Called Quest, Rick Astley and Queen

programme.title: Don't Look Back (1967)
programme.desc: DA Pennebaker's intimate film diary of Bob Dylan's 1965 tour of Britain, chronicling reactions as his tunes caught the nation off-guard, but ultimately won him critical acclaim and worldwide fame and fortune. Featuring interviews with various Sixties cult figures, including the man himself, his then-girlfriend Joan Baez and poet Allen Ginsberg

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Christopher Sykes interviews Bob Dylan, in a recording made in 1987 while the musician was filming Hearts of Fire. Their encounter reveals a side of the legendary songwriter that is rarely seen - direct, playful and fully engaged in a conversation touching on acting, music and politics

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: In 1979, Bob Dylan released Slow Train Coming, an album of strictly devotional songs and declared he had found God in Christianity. For the following two years, accompanied by the finest musicians and gospel singers, he toured with a repertoire solely of songs expressing his new-found faith. A film was made of one of those performances, though never released until now. This first showing is enhanced by a series of sermons between the songs, all specially written for the film and preached by Oscar-nominated actor Michael Shannon. The result is Bob Dylan's gospel service combining the 'then' of the gig with the 'now' of the message of The Preacher

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances from the 1960s and '70s, featuring Bob Dylan, Kate Bush, Gil Scott-Heron, Kris Kristofferson, Labi Siffre, Roy Harper, Janis Ian, Gilbert O'Sullivan and Michael Chapman, as well as a duet between Graham Nash and David Crosby

programme.title: Sings Dylan 2
programme.desc: Cover versions of Bob Dylan songs from the 1960s to the present, sourced from the BBC archives. Featuring classic tracks old and new, plus a few surprises. Performers include Joan Baez, Siouxsie and the Banshees, the Hollies, Adele, Julie Felix, Richie Havens, Bryan Ferry and KT Tunstall

programme.title: Don't Look Back (1967)
programme.desc: DA Pennebaker's intimate film diary of Bob Dylan's 1965 tour of Britain, chronicling reactions as his tunes caught the nation off-guard, but ultimately won him critical acclaim and worldwide fame and fortune. Featuring interviews with various Sixties cult figures, including the man himself, his then-girlfriend Joan Baez and poet Allen Ginsberg

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dan Snow unravels the mystery of the lost years of 400-800AD, looking at the demise of civilisation in Britain following the collapse of the Roman Empire, and revealing how Christianity transformed Ireland into a cultural powerhouse

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The presenter navigates through Ukraine and Hungary, taking in the culture and talking to locals. He travels to Budapest on the Danube, where he makes his debut on the catwalk. In Kiev he meets a Leeds market trader who has married the daughter of Ukraine's prime minister, and in Yalta he finds himself on a terrifying fairground ride

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise gets far too close to Peter. unaware of the terrible truth about him lurking beneath the surface. Karina tries to find new evidence for the investigation. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The police take Johannes to the station, and Louise fears that the truth about her and Peter Vinge will be revealed. Peter meets his ex-wife. In Danish

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
programme.desc: George Smiley's quiet retirement from the secret service is interrupted when he is asked to undertake an undercover assignment, and discovers all is not well at the Circus. It transpires that a highly influential mole has been operating for some time. Classic 1970s espionage drama, starring Alec Guinness as John le Carre's spymaster

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy
programme.desc: George Smiley is told a story about spies and invited to go forwards - and backwards - over some very old ground. Second part of the classic 1970s espionage drama, starring Alec Guinness, with Michael Jayston and Hywel Bennett

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: A major part of the investigation is stopped in its tracks, leading prime suspect Troels Hartmann to come under further scrutiny - and his political opponent Bremer takes advantage. Meanwhile, Lund and Meyer continue their own line of inquiry, and Theis's emotional state takes a turn for the worse. Drama, starring Lars Mikkelsen and Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund encounters a potentially deadly situation, but no one knows her whereabouts. Meanwhile, the mystery of the murderer's identity looks like it may be resolved, Bremer delivers a blow to Hartmann's political career, and Pernille finds herself in need of help. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund refuses to believe her superiors' insistence that the murder has been solved, and considers continuing her investigation alone. Hartmann prepares to reveal embarrassing details as Bremer makes a gesture of goodwill, while Pernille and Theis attempt a reconciliation. In Danish

programme.desc: Today's rookies Dylan and Izzy have an eye for clothes and a burning desire to be fashion designers! They join presenter Alex Riley on a journey to see what a life in fashion can be like. First up they head to The London College of Fashion to team up with Course Director and Designer Claudine Rousseau to start creating their own garments out of recycled clothes - affordable and sustainable. They continue to learn how the business works when they meet top Fashion Designer Giles Deacon. Giles guides them through the nuts and bolts of blocking their designs and pattern cutting and finally the construction of the garment. Then their creations hit the catwalk at Oxford Fashion Week!

We meet 12-year-old Sami, who with the help of his dad, has built a car that runs on drill power. Sami’s family have Syrian heritage, and they celebrate learning the best of both cultures. programme.title: Mimi and Co
programme.desc: In the last episode of the series, Mimi enlists her brother Leo’s help to fulfil a lifelong dream: to win a Blue Peter badge. She teaches Leo to rollerblade, they upcycle a mirror together and draw portraits of each other, only to discover they are too old to apply for a badge! We meet proud badge holders Iris and Aidan, and find out all about the Chinese Mid- Autumn Festival, which they celebrate with their mum and dad. Luckily for Mimi, Blue Peter’s very own Mwaksy invites Mimi to co-host Fan Club Live, and there is a special surprise for her in the post.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

They even use some binoculars! Over in Ireland, Rosie’s brother Donnacha picks some colourful flowers at the garden centre to take home and show him. Back at home, Fionn is seeing more than his lunch, but what else can he see? In Rosie’s garden they plant their flowers and are inspired to finger-paint using all the colours from the rainbow, while Fionn’s family does a treasure hunt in the garden. programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, River, Araiya and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag are numbered building blocks, and all the parents and little ones have different objects and toys that are themed around numbers. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate number-themed objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The traditional children’s nursery rhyme One, Two, Buckle My Shoe features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring numbers too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog gets a delivery that arrives in a big box and the squirrels discover that cardboard can be very interesting

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Chloe’s been feeling under the weather, so it’s up to Topsy to make her feel better.

programme.title: Kiri & Lou
programme.desc: Lou eats too much sugar and runs amok at a poetry recital.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. tries to keep up with a family of Huggy Rollers as they roll along together. B.O.T. ends up encased in a snowball, but the snow melts away when the Rollers give it a big warm hug!

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day explains about the challenges faced by the newborns of animals such as wildebeest, penguins and caiman, and how they rely on their parents to get them around

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special walk through the town that starts in the daytime but finishes with a torch game in the dark. On the way they spot a spiky tree, have a close-up look at a daisy, light up their reflectors in the dark, and even make sounds like the sea!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Asnath and Tianah perform alongside a trio of Shebas for Sister Sledge’s iconic ‘We Are Family’. They open the episode next to a statue of an African-Caribbean mother holding her child. The statue was made to encourage women of the future to achieve their dreams and support each other, just like a family! This is why the song, ‘We are Family’ was picked. Asnath and Tianah teach the sign for ‘family’ so you can join in.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Otus gets a new remote-controlled truck, and Timmy resorts to a number of increasingly reckless tricks to play with it, until disaster strikes and the toy breaks

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join eight-month-old Myles and Iffah as they discover the world around them and begin to gain some independence. Both children start the day with some breakfast. Myles’ mum is back to work for the first time since he was born, so he’s spending the day with dad. After breakfast, Iffah and her brother Walli play together - he does a drawing while she plays with her toy duck. Meanwhile, Myles and his dad heads to a local park, where they look at the lake and see lots of different birds. Iffah’s family go to an indoor play area, where Iffah has fun playing in the ball pool. When Myles and his dad arrive at great grandma’s house, Myles reads a book with grandma and even turns the pages all on his own! After they join up with mum, they all visit a quarry to feed some fish. Iffah finishes the day with her family at a petting farm, where she gets to stroke an alpaca.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Angel invites Tig to a sleepover. At night the visiting youngster finds herself all alone with everyone else asleep, calling upon Woolly to help her survive the night

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Groan Up is having a very busy party, and it's hard for the Olobobs to find their way home

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Sienna's father is from Nigeria and he has taught her a unique way of making Nigerian Jollof rice, which she demonstrates here

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: When Jess takes Evie for her first school visit, Waffle goes along too - on the condition that he doesn't sing. He tries his best to behave, but it's hard for a Wonder Dog!

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Finlay folds a fantastic paper plane, and Timmy becomes upset when his efforts to copy him fall flat, until his friend's creation gets stuck in a tree

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly organises a sleepover at the Big Hub to raise money to fix the leaky roof, but when everyone gathers that night Magnus reveals he is scared of the dark

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo loves the Book Animals, but when he gets a new book with a spider in it, Pablo thinks the spider looks scary. However, it turns out the spider is a bit frightened as well

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘star’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of stars – a yellow star, a bright one, a pointy star and a jingle star. We see Dee and a friend sprinkle stars, and Dee wishes on a shooting star! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The discovery of some invisible ink leads to all manner of tricks being played on the Alphablocks

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: It's just another day at the Furchester Hotel, that is until the Furball, a bouncing furry monster ball, arrives in a flurry of bouncing and chanting! Soon all the Furchesters are jumping and chanting 'Furball!' too. Elmo asks why they are all shouting 'Furball' and Furgus says that it's because the Furball has arrived, it's the best game ever, whenever he turns up at the hotel they all play a game of 'Furball', he's very excited and goes to get his Furball equipment bag and everything is there except his Furball shoes. Furgus and Funella go to look for his shoes and tell Phoebe and Elmo to keep an eye on the Furball and not let him out of their sight. Just at that moment, the Furball disappears, only for them to spot him up on the balcony, they're soon chasing him all over the hotel. Phoebe decides to look in her Dad's Furball bag and finds a Furball net, they hear boinging and get ready to pounce, but they catch a very suspicious looking rabbit instead. Cookie Monster then comes alon

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Bitz & Bob

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Sucky Sprinkler flies from flower to flower using its nose to gather seeds. The Sucky Sprinkler sows and waters them too! B.O.T. follows close behind, but finding a way through the craters of Spacey Glowy World isn’t easy.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes shoe shopping with his friend

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how space rockets work and how they take astronauts into space. We discover how thrust and rocket fuel work, when a rocket engine blasts off from the launch pad and into space, and learn about the different parts of the rocket and what they are for. Next, we find out about the special computers on-board that guide astronauts through space, and Maddie rides a very fast rollercoaster to show us how G-force works when a rocket launches. Finally, we find out how a rocket lands safely to bring the astronauts back to Earth.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy tries to complete his chores quickly so he can go outside to play, but ends up making more mess and getting into trouble

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Kobi is painting a wall but cannot work out which colour to use

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The twins are excited about visiting their new school for the first time, but wonder how they are going to get there each day

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon recalls the first time he met Waffle as a pup and the pair share memories of the Wonder Dog becoming part of the Brooklyn-Bell family

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Lawrie the Little Owl has his feathers full. His four little owlets want to play hide and seek with him, but finding a good hiding spot on the old barn roof is rather tricky. Maybe he can try and blend in with the chimney? Or perhaps he could hide under gutters? He’d better find somewhere suitable, and quickly. His owlets are expert seekers!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet and her family are off on a animal adventure! They visit a safari park where they join the lions for a spot of lunch from the safety of their safari bus. Later, Amanpreet turns her hand to zookeeping. The rhino’s need a mud bath and Amanpreet is ready to help - it’s a mucky job, but someone has to do it! She also gets to feed the giraffes, but will the gentle giants be tempted by her cabbage buffet?

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Reau, Alfie, Charlie, Ollie, Otis, River and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Magic Hands

programme.desc: Kari hosts a talent show to demonstrate the Treasure Champs' many skills, but Barry needs some help in recognising that his unique talent is worthy of celebrating

programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.desc: Lotta's birthday arrives, and Lola buys her the best present she can find - but decides she would like to keep it for herself

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee insists on wearing his favourite Action Ape outfit all afternoon, no matter what games he is playing. However, when the outfit gets dirty, Tee is heartbroken

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small is unhappy with his drawing, so he finds a place to practise in peace. Major Clanger discovers some wonderful wall pictures in an underground cave and invites everyone to see them. It turns out that Small has seen the pictures before

programme.title: In the Night Garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
