programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits Staffordshire to investigate a damp and dingy two-bed terrace, before heading to Rochdale, Greater Manchester, where he discovers a neglected detached house with some beautiful period features. Meanwhile, Martel Maxwell goes to Hertfordshire to find out just what can be done with a pair of old garages

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: A locksmith sees a chance to make quick gains when he is put in charge of a London hospital - and pockets nearly half a million pounds by overcharging the hospital for supplies. Elsewhere, an NHS manager dreams up a scam to siphon over £800,000 of NHS money into his own account. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The emergency services attend a baby who has cut his throat on a glass. Plus, a woman is knocked to the ground and trampled after a dog spooks horses. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie's team appreciation gift exchange leads to some big decisions, while Ruhma supports Sid on the day of Lewis' wake. Jimmi helps a struggling student to mend his relationship with his strictly vegan ex-girlfriend

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Andy, Stuart, Alice and Charlotte vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Scottish Borders with a couple who want their £450,000 budget to buy them an equestrian property in the country. Jules also visits the dramatic ruins of Melrose Abbey, where the heart of Scotland's medieval king Robert the Bruce is believed to be buried

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, inviting the questions of a virtual audience from around the UK. On her panel are Secretary of State for Transport Grant Shapps MP, Shadow Secretary for International Trade Emily Thornberry MP, Executive Chairman of the National Care Association Nadra Ahmed, poet, novelist and playwright Kate Clanchy, and broadcaster and journalist Tom Newton Dunn

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned in St Margaret's at Cliffe, Kent, Dalton-in-Furness, Cumbria and Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley follows the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, featuring the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A woman gives birth on the M5 with the help of her husband and an emergency call handler, while a family's car is hit by an oak tree during a storm

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks to West Dale Bay, starting at the Dale Peninsula in South Pembrokeshire, where he learns about an invasion that changed the course of history. He also helps carve a seahorse and takes part in a cliff rescue - as the casualty. Wildlife expert Lizzie Daly gets a close look at the birds who visit the area and Onkar Singh Purewal explores how walking can aid general wellbeing

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurs prepare to capitalise on Valentine's Day, with Bridgend-based Scott ordering in 1,500 hand-decorated love-themed doughnuts. At Mallows Beauty, Laura and Ronnie are flat out trying to meet demand on their Valantine's Day gift box, while in Cardiff, Caribbean take-away owner Charlotte comes up with a new idea to sell - romantic meals for two in portable pop-up igloos

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Harry and David Rich help Bath resident Mina, who wants to turn her characterless, overgrown lawn into a Persian paradise. They have a budget of £2,700 to create something Mina will be proud of, and which is evocative of her childhood in the Middle East, including colourful and aromatic planting, stunning tiles, fruit trees and an Iranian-inspired water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Paul Martin visit places including Dorset, Devon and Somerset. Along the way, Paul investigates the obscure, Somerset-based Victorian inventor of unmanned powered flight, while Raj finds out about The Bristol Bus Boycott, a 1960s struggle against racial discrimination. Both experts make profitable finds, with a mule-based fairground ride and an exquisite English oak candle box the star lots at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mila worries about protecting Kioni as Kathy orders Bobby to look after her, while Vinny wonders about his brother's mystery woman but Kheerat refuses to kiss and tell. He later runs into Sharon, who mentions she's free all day but Kheerat isn't keen - until he sees Gray and suddenly changes his mind. Keegan leaves Sonia with some food for thought

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A London-based entrepreneur with an assortment of naturally derived sugar alternatives hopes he can pull off a sweet deal, while a couple face a roasting over their optimistic projections for their vitamin-infused coffee. Multimillionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are also approached by an inventor who hopes to fix a deal with her portable children's suction mats and consider investing in a business selling innovative eco-cleaning products. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: What Are We Feeding Our Kids?
programme.desc: Across the world childhood obesity rates have risen tenfold in 50 years. In the UK 21 per cent of children are living with obesity when they leave primary school - the highest it's ever been. Ultra-processed food is cheap and convenient, and accounts for the majority of young people's calorie intake - 64 per cent for children and 67 per cent for teenagers. Yet alarmingly, scientists know very little about exactly how it affects youngsters' bodies. Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates the impact of ultra-processed food, as he undergoes a gruelling self-experiment that even shocks the scientists

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, inviting the questions of a virtual audience from around the UK. On her panel are Secretary of State for Transport Grant Shapps MP, Shadow Secretary for International Trade Emily Thornberry MP, Executive Chairman of the National Care Association Nadra Ahmed, poet, novelist and playwright Kate Clanchy, and broadcaster and journalist Tom Newton Dunn

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: The Road to Partition
programme.desc: Part one of two. On June 22, 1921, King George V and Queen Mary arrived in Belfast for the official opening of the first Northern Ireland parliament. Fearful for their lives, they had come to a city scarred by bitter sectarian violence. One hundred years on, this is the story of the dramatic events that led to the partition of Ireland - one that continues to reverberate to the present day

programme.desc: A review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests. Presented by Mark Carruthers

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, inviting the questions of a virtual audience from around the UK. On her panel are Secretary of State for Transport Grant Shapps MP, Shadow Secretary for International Trade Emily Thornberry MP, Executive Chairman of the National Care Association Nadra Ahmed, poet, novelist and playwright Kate Clanchy, and broadcaster and journalist Tom Newton Dunn

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic heptathlon champion Denise Lewis, rugby league great Paul Sculthorpe, former England striker Kevin Phillips and British javelin star Goldie Sayers join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned in St Margaret's at Cliffe, Kent, Dalton-in-Furness, Cumbria and Great Barr, Birmingham

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley follows the work of investigators in the NHS Counter Fraud Authority, featuring the case of a hospital worker who stole more than £1,000,000 worth of surgical equipment to sell online

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A woman gives birth on the M5 with the help of her husband and an emergency call handler, while a family's car is hit by an oak tree during a storm

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where two teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction on a budget of £300 each. Natasha monitors their progress and also takes a historic look at the semi-precious gemstone Blue John

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Olivia, AF, Gavin and Zoe vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: In St Albans, Hertfordshire, Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. The buyers have up to £800 to spend on something to celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary, but cannot agree on what this should be. Can a sculptor, a ceramicist or a painter resolve their dilemma?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a retired couple search for a new home in rural Somerset with a budget of £700,000, and firm ideas on the vision for their perfect country home. Along the way, Jonnie masters the thrilling sport of land yachting at Brean Sands, where thanks to a prevailing wind he picks up the pace along this picturesque stretch of beach

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: After an epic tour of the south and south west, Raj Bisram and Paul Martin's shopping and sale-room the duo hope to acquire anything 'going for a sonnet'. Their shopping and sale-room extravaganza concludes at a Palladian mansion in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Host Sue Barker and team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell bid farewell to the sports quiz as they are joined by British tennis great Tim Henman, former Aston Villa striker Dion Dublin, Ashes-winning England cricket captain Michael Vaughan and ex-Wales rugby union forward Scott Quinnell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Sue Johnston, Alice Levine and Bob Mortimer join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds including Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies. Savvy viewers may have noted that after 12 series, both Mitchell and Mack are running woefully short of truthful bizarre experiences to recount of themselves

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat enjoys making Suki squirm about his dalliance with Sharon and as he dashes out, she gets a call from Jags. Sonia tells Tiffany she is taking it step by step with her dad, while Bobby and Iqra are both battling with their feelings, and Iqra has doubts as to whether Mila really cares

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Comedy actor David Mitchell hosts the satirical quiz, with guest panellists including comedian Jane Godley joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: The broadcaster takes to the skies with a flight in a spitfire, while This Time co-presenter Jennie comes face to face with her online troll. Plus, new band Cayman perform live on their show. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Bryn Williams and Liv Burt, plus guest Michael Ball. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom maisonette in London, a house in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and a mid-terrace in Goldenhill, Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team hunt for bargains at Exeter's Westpoint Arena. Helping the red and blue teams spend £300 on three items are experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps an Oxford couple find a new home on the West Sussex coast, and dons protective gear to join a conservation project protecting nature's most productive workers

programme.title: Missing Link (2019)
programme.desc: Fearless explorer Sir Lionel Frost is approached by fur-covered, eight-foot-tall creature Mr Link who, tired of living a solitary existence in the Pacific Northwest, wants to find his long-lost relatives in the fabled valley of Shangri-La. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Zach Galifianakis, Zoe Saldana, David Walliams and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ricky Wilson from the Kaiser Chiefs, as they assist a paralympic athlete and her boyfriend from Sheffield in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a chef, a life coach, and a salesman

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Special edition featuring Mike Nolan, Toyah Willcox, Dr Linda Papadopoulos, Dr Zoe Williams, Nathan Caton, Suzi Ruffell, Anthony Costa and Shaun Williamson

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty heads to the clinic seeking revenge for his mother's poor treatment, accompanied by Jan posing as a potential patient - but he receives a distressing gets a call from Jade that changes everything - Bibi has been rushed into the ED after an overdose. Tina is concerned that something is not right with Jacob, who confesses his true feelings for her after a stressful day overwhelms him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. Fenisha's difficult situation is made even more complicated when her former fiance starts working at Holby as a locum registrar. Rash puts his medical training to the test, and Marty channels his anger into a good cause after his mother's suicide attempt

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend 2021
programme.desc: Greg James introduces highlights from this year's virtual festival, including performances by Ed Sheeran, AJ Tracey and Royal Blood recorded in front of iconic backdrops around the UK. The programme also includes highlights of a special show at the BBC Radio Theatre, featuring Celeste, Mabel, Anne-Marie, Wolf Alice and London Grammar

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the greatest Premier League champions

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Wednesday's business in the House of Lords, including questions to Government ministers, statement on Covid-19 and a debate on the general principles of the Dormant Assets Bill, from Wednesday 26 May

programme.title: Wednesday in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the day's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the Cabinet Office minister Michael Gove and his team

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the urgent question in the House of Commons, tabled by Shadow Health Secretary Jon Ashworth, on the Government's handling of the Covid-19 pandemic and the Department of Health's level of preparedness

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the urgent question in the House of Commons, tabled by Shadow International Trade Secretary Emily Thornberry, on the UK's proposed tariff offer to the Australian government on agricultural exports.

programme.title: House of Commons

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches South Australia as she continues her 12,000km journey across the continent. She drives along the Explorers Way, a route through the Outback mapped out by a pioneer in 1862, to the world's most isolated pub, where a small plane takes her on to the ancient landscape of the Painted Hills. In Adelaide, she climbs the roof of the Oval sports stadium and meets a women's cycling team, before ending her journey in wine country

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Anne Clegg and her mother Janet were first reunited nearly a decade ago. She reveals the impact their successful mother-daughter relationship has had on her life. The team also catches up with grandmother Alice Jones, who was originally looking for her younger brother Sam. And there is an update on Sharon Harte and her sisters, Carole, Terri and Sandra

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a 'pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James's guests are Texas frontwoman Sharleen Spiteri, and Formula 1 pundit David Coulthard, who joins live from Monaco. There are recipes for mackerel, knickerbocker glory and panna cotta, while chefs Gareth Ward and Galton Blackiston also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have recipes for holiday favourites, including indulgent French toast served two ways, as well as Lisa's Spanish-style stew served with John's aioli. Plus, seasoned tofu steaks accompanied by a rainbow salad and homemade sweet chilli sauce, and finally peaches and cream pies and a roasted peach daiquiri for dessert

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the Camargue, a distinct and diverse region located south of Arles between the Mediterranean and the two arms of the Rhone delta. James begins his exploration at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns all about the Camargue bull. He then barbeques a local fish, which he serves with squash chutney for the family. Later, Michelin-starred chef Armand Arnal shows James around his impressive gardens and lets him pick ingredients for his next dish - stuffed courgette flowers

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents coverage of the Pinnacle Stakes and the John O'Gaunt Stakes from Haydock Park, plus racing from Beverley and Chester. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Natalie Green, Mick Fitzgerald and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Adam Garcia, Carol Decker, Holly Walsh and Rylan Clark-Neal pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special edition of the classic game show, with Gemma Atkinson, Karen Hauer and Josh Widdicombe all trying to 'say what they see' as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Masked Dancer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a contest in which Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall, Mo Gilligan and Oti Mabuse must guess the identities of 12 famous faces performing in elaborate costumes. programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia reaches South Australia as she continues her 12,000km journey across the continent. She drives along the Explorers Way, a route through the Outback mapped out by a pioneer in 1862, to the world's most isolated pub, where a small plane takes her on to the ancient landscape of the Painted Hills. In Adelaide, she climbs the roof of the Oval sports stadium and meets a women's cycling team, before ending her journey in wine country

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next

Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the Camargue, a distinct and diverse region located south of Arles between the Mediterranean and the two arms of the Rhone delta. James begins his exploration at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns all about the Camargue bull. He then barbeques a local fish, which he serves with squash chutney for the family. Later, Michelin-starred chef Armand Arnal shows James around his impressive gardens and lets him pick ingredients for his next dish - stuffed courgette flowers

programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. To solve the mystery, Foyle must penetrate the closed ranks of the airforce. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo gets involved in a row with the estate manager when she accidentally leads a group of schoolchildren across Lord Ashfordly's land, disrupting the local hunt. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Nick takes a risk by returning home to look for Sam. Emma meets a handsome stranger. Tyrone feels the strain of supporting two households

programme.title: Charade (1963)
programme.desc: A distraught widow learns her husband was murdered for his part in a $250,000 robbery. The haul has gone missing, and his former accomplices are soon on her tail in the hunt for the missing proceeds. Not knowing where to turn, she takes up the offer of help from a dashing stranger, but is uncertain whether she can trust him. Romantic thriller, with Audrey Hepburn, Cary Grant, Walter Matthau and James Coburn

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls asleep during a flight from Paris to London, while a female passenger is murdered by a poisoned dart, leaving few clues to the killer's identity. Poirot soon discovers she was a money-lender, which may provide a motive for the killing. The investigation is turned on its head, however, when the victim's illegitimate daughter comes forward to claim the inheritance. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with David Firth, Jenny Downham and John Bleasdale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: The arrival of Miss Lambe's forbidden love causes Charlotte to question her opinion of Sidney. Clara witnesses a moment between Esther and Sir Edward that raises her suspicions about their relationship, with devastating consequences for Esther. Period drama, starring Rose Williams and Theo James

programme.desc: Ida is dismayed when handyman Jim Barnes is given his marching orders and devises a plan to get his job back

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a dilemma when a murder investigation leads him to the radar station where his missing son was working on a highly classified operation - just as Hitler launches a major offensive on Britain's RAF airfields. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Julian Ovenden, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Calf, Roger Allam and Anton Lesser

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The fossil record shows that the arapaima fish has roamed the waters of the Amazon River since the Jurassic age, and uses its bony tongue to crush prey against the roof of its mouth before swallowing. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade encountered the species once before and came out second best, so this time he is taking no chances as he investigates every aspect of this huge creature

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The super-powered trio are sent to Italy to find out why a number of Interpol agents seemingly committed suicide. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Moriarty seeks revenge on Holmes for foiling his plan to sell the Mona Lisa, and the pair ultimately fight their final battle at the Reichenbach Falls in Switzerland. programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A man-eating tiger is on the loose - but the big cat attack is just the first step in a plot to take over the country. Adventure, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg, with Ronnie Barker

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle considers his future in CI5 after it transpires the father of his fiancee is under surveillance by the unit, suspected of being part of an international drug-smuggling ring. Patricia Hodge guest stars, with Martin Shaw

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: A young rider is lucky to be alive after plunging down an unstable mineshaft on his motorbike. Team Salvage is in Kalgoorlie, the gold mining capital of Australia, to try and retrieve the damaged bike. They have five hours and a 4,500-dollar budget to compete this highly dangerous retrieval. The whole area is unstable and the bike is jammed 10-metres down a shaft.

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Actress Sally Phillips, musician Rick Wakeman, professional snowboarder Jenny Jones and impressionist Alistair McGowan pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser, as Bradley Walsh hosts another star-studded edition of the show. The celebrities must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Scottish firm making cashmere for the world's finest fashion houses. Plus, a look at how a jigsaw puzzle is made, the family of fishermen producing Welsh delicacies of cockles and laver bread, and how a quintessentially British teapot is made

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. Thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatrick's own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noel's life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a car right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noel's colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

Most have the aerobic fitness age of someone at least 20 years older. programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Violent hurricanes and severe drought have decimated once fertile areas of Central America, with the region's rural poor worst affected. Hunger and child malnourishment is widespread, prompting an exodus of migrants to the United States. Reporter Guillermo Galdos tracks the journey of Gonzalo, who desperate to give his family a better life, risks his own by employing a network of people smugglers to illegally enter the US. The film reveals how climate migrants have become a valuable commodity in a booming trade and in a region controlled by crime cartels, some are paying the ultimate price

programme.desc: The battle to save Thailand's wild tigers from poachers and smugglers, who have made millions from their sinister and sometimes deadly trade. Reporter Jonathan Miller travels to the last safe haven of the near-extinct Indochinese tiger and meets the rangers and conservationists fighting to protect the endangered tiger. Thai wildlife enforcement agencies are determined to crack down on the illegal trade in both live and dead tigers. However, they are struggling against a lucrative and murky industry that supplies an insatiable demand from Vietnam and China for trophies and quack medicine

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner: 10 Years and a Lovely Bit of Squirrel
programme.desc: A celebration of Robert Popper's comedy Friday Night Dinner, to mark its 10-year anniversary. The sitcom, which coined the catchphrase 'lovely bit of squirrel', centred on the fortunes of the eccentric Goodman family and their even more surreal neighbour Jim. With behind-the-scenes material and never-before-seen outtakes, this documentary features interviews with stars including Tamsin Greig, Paul Ritter, Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Mark Heap and Tracy-Ann Oberman. It also includes the thoughts of celebrity fans Claudia Winkleman, Joe Thomas, David Baddiel and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: After two gruelling years during which Johnny Vegas has seen his plans for his glampsite hit by every problem imaginable, he is finally ready for the grand unveiling. Unfortunately, it's in front of his harshest critics - his family and friends. With the final vehicles arriving, there's time for Johnny to deceive his assistant Bev one more time, before he presents her with the 1950s camper van he bought and restored as a gift. programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon returns to offer more members of the public help with innovative design projects that should revolutionise their homes without calling for them to move. He begins in Puckaun, Co Tipperary, where teacher Darragh Egan, his partner Sarah Lovett and his 70-year-old mother Mary are seeking to convert their shared 1960s bungalow into two, self-contained wings. One will be for Mary, the other for Darragh, Sarah and their infant son Donagh. However, demolition delays caused by the existing building's foot-thick floors and walls leave local contractor Brendan Cormican and quality surveyor Patricia Power struggling to square Dermot's vision with the 200,000-euro budget

programme.title: Chronic Pain: How to Live With It

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: The adventurer meets more people who have turned their backs on the rat-race and set up home in some of the most remote locations on Earth. This week, Ben travels to the vast forests of the Rhodope Mountains in Bulgaria to join Miriam and Peter, a couple who have chosen life as nomads, hiking through some of the world's most striking wildernesses. They are educated but unemployed and homeless - and it's just the way they like it. Having previously walked the length of New Zealand in 2016, Ben now finds the duo on an intrepid journey across South Eastern Europe

programme.title: Eight Days That Made Rome
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes recalls the time that marked Rome's symbolic break with its 1,000-year pagan past - the day in 337AD that Emperor Constantine the Great was baptised a Christian. It was a moment of profound significance not just for the empire, but for the history of the world and one of its major religions. Constantine was one of the last great Roman emperors to rule over a united empire, giving it a new capital - Constantinople, today known as Istanbul - a city which would one day eclipse Rome as the greatest city on Earth. Dramatisations featuring Ian Beattie and Gary Finan bring the key moments to life

programme.desc: The presenter visits Budapest, and enjoys three days exploring one of the most popular city break destinations in eastern Europe. Plus, he also samples the local food

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras follow a train route from Bergen to Oslo, through the extraordinary mountains of southern Norway in winter. This super-fast train travels daily to the country's capital, and first stop is Dale - a place where Norway's most famous knitters design and craft the country's famous jumpers. The final stop is Sandvika, where Monet painted around 30 scenes. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Revisiting the musical highs of 1992 in the company of Shaun Ryder, Jayne Middlemiss, Bez, Paul Gambaccini and others. It was the year Wet Wet Wet, Right Said Fred, Shakespears Sister and the Shamen dominated the charts, but what was the biggest selling single of the year?

programme.title: Mariah: The Diva, The Demons, The Drama
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of Mariah Carey's life and career, offering first hand accounts of her physical and emotional breakdown in the early 2000s. In 2003, Mariah was the most successful female recording artist of all time, but her life began to fall apart when she started to struggle with her mental health. Years later, with her diagnosis of bipolar disorder in 2012, Mariah began to put her life and career back on track

Hapless teenager Sam Witwicky is caught in the middle of the ensuing conflict as he holds the key to discovering the location of the device. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Shia LaBeouf and Megan Fox

programme.title: Red Sparrow (2018)
programme.desc: Ballerina Dominika Egorova is recruited to a Russian intelligence service, where she is forced to use her body as a weapon. Her first mission, targeting a CIA agent, threatens to unravel the security of both nations. Spy thriller, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Joel Edgerton and Matthias Schoenaerts

programme.title: Last Action Hero (1993)
programme.desc: A boy watching an action movie is transported into the film by a magic ticket and meets its hero figure, who refuses to believe he is a fictional character. Matters take a turn for the worse when the screen villain gets hold of the ticket and crosses over into the real world - where bad guys can win. Comedy adventure, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Austin O'Brien, Charles Dance, F Murray Abraham and Anthony Quinn

programme.title: Dude, Where's My Car? (2000)
programme.desc: Two friends wake up with the realisation they have absolutely no memory of the events of the night before. They set about retracing their steps - only to stumble across a host of crazy characters, a flock of marauding ostriches and a fanatical alien-worshipping cult. Comedy, starring Seann William Scott, Ashton Kutcher, Jennifer Garner and Kristy Swanson

programme.title: Donovan's Reef (1963)
programme.desc: An American soldier settles down with a friend and a kindly doctor on a South Sea island to relax in the aftermath of the Second World War. The arrival of the medic's snobbish daughter throws their idyllic life into turmoil, leaving them struggling to make her father seem respectable. Comedy drama directed by John Ford, starring John Wayne, Lee Marvin, Jack Warden, Elizabeth Allen and Cesar Romero

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)
programme.desc: Two Navy officers are disgraced in the aftermath of the attack on Pearl Harbour, one for a disastrous engagement with the enemy and the other for a drunken brawl after learning of his wife's death. They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles (1959)
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson are asked to protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskerville family, who is believed to be the target of a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Hammer version of Arthur Conan Doyle's mystery, starring Peter Cushing, Andre Morrell, Christopher Lee, John Le Mesurier and Miles Malleson

programme.title: Star Trek (2009)
programme.desc: The trainee crew members of the starship Enterprise embark on their maiden flight, which pits them against a Romulan commander from the future who is planning a horrific revenge on those he blames for a tragedy in his own time. Sci-fi adventure based on the original TV series, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto, Eric Bana, Zoe Saldana and Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Hobbit Interview Special
programme.desc: Peter Jackson discusses returning to the world of The Lord of the Rings for his fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's story

programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey (2012)
programme.desc: Unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins is pulled out of his comfortable life when Gandalf the wizard volunteers him for a dangerous quest. He joins a party of dwarves who are on a mission to reclaim their lost homeland, which has been taken over by a dragon. When the group journeys into the Misty Mountains, Bilbo becomes separated from the dwarves and falls into a cave, where he finds himself confronted by Gollum and forced to play a riddle game. It is set in Middle-earth 60 years before the events of The Lord of the Rings and is the first part of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage, James Nesbitt, Ken Stott, Cate Blanchett, Ian Holm and Christopher Lee

programme.title: Daybreakers (2009)
programme.desc: In the near future, vampires have taken over the world, with the few remaining humans farmed as a food source. An undead scientist tries to develop synthetic blood, only to run into a revolutionary group claiming to have discovered a cure for vampirism. Thriller, starring Ethan Hawke, Willem Dafoe and Sam Neill

programme.title: Paranormal Activity: The Marked Ones (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager investigates the murder of a neighbour, which leads him to discover evidence of black magic. He begins to experience strange and disturbing changes and realises he may be succumbing to a sinister curse placed on him by a coven of witches and their demonic allies. Horror sequel, starring Andrew Jacobs and Jorge Diaz

programme.title: The Core (2003)
programme.desc: A chain of bizarre and deadly incidents in several countries leads scientists to the conclusion that the Earth's core has stopped spinning. As chaos erupts around the world, experts hatch a plan to halt the destruction and save the human race - but it involves venturing deep below the planet's surface to detonate a nuclear bomb. Sci-fi thriller, starring Aaron Eckhart, Hilary Swank, Stanley Tucci and DJ Qualls

programme.title: Mother's Day (2016)
programme.desc: As Mother's Day approaches, an array of seemingly unconnected characters all find themselves reconsidering their relationships with their mothers and what motherhood mean to them. Garry Marshall's ensemble comedy in which various families go through dramatic turning points, starring Julia Roberts, Jennifer Aniston, Kate Hudson, Jason Sudeikis, Jennifer Garner and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992)
programme.desc: Hapless youngster Kevin somehow manages to catch the wrong plane and finds himself heading for New York, while his family jets off to Florida. Having got his hands on his father's credit card, he is enjoying the good life staying in a luxury hotel - until he bumps into two familiar burglars with an axe to grind. Comedy sequel, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, Brenda Fricker and Tim Curry

programme.title: Star Trek Into Darkness (2013)
programme.desc: Captain Kirk and the crew of the starship Enterprise are sent on a clandestine mission into enemy territory to track down the rogue Starfleet agent behind a terrorist attack on Earth. However, the assignment brings to light a hidden threat from within their own organisation. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Chris Pine, Zachary Quinto and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug (2013)
programme.desc: The party of dwarves and their hobbit ally continue the quest to reclaim their lost kingdom, journeying through the forest home of ancestral enemy the elves and finally facing the dragon that drove them from their home. Meanwhile, Gandalf the wizard investigates the rise of Necromancer, a mysterious dark power residing in Dol Guldur with the ability to summon the spirits of the dead. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Martin Freeman, Ian McKellen, Richard Armitage and Orlando Bloom, with Luke Evans, Evangeline Lilly and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: One Cut of the Dead (2017)
programme.desc: A film crew's attempts to make a low-budget horror movie are frustrated when they are attacked by 'real-life' zombies. Comedy horror, starring Takayuki Hamatsu. In Japanese, with English subtitles

programme.title: Paranormal Activity: The Ghost Dimension (2015)
programme.desc: A man moves into a new house along with his wife, daughter and younger brother, and finds an old camcorder and a box of videotapes. The siblings start watching the tapes, which turn out to be home movies shot by the property's previous owners, but by doing so, they unleash evil spirits into the world. Horror sequel, starring Chris J Murray and Brit Shaw

programme.desc: Ben discovers a shocking secret about Riley's new love Fitch, while things heat up between Danny and girlfriend Milena, and Tucker seizes an opportunity to impress his boss Chase. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Tucker are keen to throw Bonnie out of the building when she sets up her new apartment across the hall from the boys, and decide to throw a huge, noisy party to annoy her. Meanwhile, Danny asks Riley for advice on how to split with Milena, but winds up in a compromising position. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau and Melissa Peterman

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre and Rainbow throw a party to reveal the gender of their unborn child. Dre is keen to name the baby, but his wife and mother aren't fans of his name choice. Zoey's plan to have an anti-Valentine's Day with her friend Shelly is thwarted by Andre Jr asking Shelly for a date. Comedy, starring Anthony Anderson and Marcus Scribner

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley invites Ben to be her date at a work party, but his efforts to prove he is dependable fall short, and Danny hopes to come to her rescue instead. Meanwhile, Tucker schemes to go out with a model, and Bonnie attempts to get to know a handsome stranger. Starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Newcomer Eloise sets pulses racing as she joins the gang in Suffolk, and Inga is left feeling unnerved at the idea of a single girl around Miles following their dramatic break-up

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Peter Rabbit (2018)
programme.desc: A mischievous rabbit has the run of a farm and its vegetable patch when the owner unexpectedly dies. When a relative inherits the property, he strives to rid his land of the rabbit and his woodland friends - but his feelings for a nature-loving woman complicate the battle of wits with the animal kingdom. Family comedy based on Beatrix Potter's stories, starring Domhnall Gleeson and Rose Byrne, with the voices of James Corden and Margot Robbie

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj endures an anxious day as he worries that a space probe he helped design will not function properly, while Leonard and Sheldon agree to go clothes shopping with Penny and Amy. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar, Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon becomes concerned that his comfortable lifestyle is having an impact on his professional progress and is stopping him from achieving scientific greatness, so he asks the gang to make life more difficult for him. Meanwhile, Howard invents an unflattering game about Raj. Starring Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon are forced to defend themselves against criticism from an online bully after publishing a physics paper. Meanwhile, the girls confront embarrassing moments from their pasts during a night in. Comedy, guest starring Stephen Hawking, with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard receives shocking news as everyone prepares for the re-opening of the comic-book store. Penny tries to teach Sheldon how to 'let it go', but she is unable to follow her own advice when she learns something infuriating about Amy. Comedy, guest starring Nathan Fillion, with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Penny agree to participate in an unusual experiment, and Leonard, Amy, Raj and Emily spend the evening trying to escape from a 'zombie'. Elsewhere, Howard and Bernadette run into trouble at the airport after returning from Mrs Wolowitz's funeral. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Trouble arises when Amy learns that Sheldon has applied for a one-way mission to colonise Mars. Meanwhile, Leonard surprises Penny with a gift, and Raj decides to snoop around Emily's flat after she is called back to work during their evening together. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tests a newly invented device after hearing that it can help him to read people's emotions. Leonard isn't happy when he finds out that Penny's brother is coming to town so that she can help him to find a job. Raj gathers his ex-girlfriends together so that they can give him some dating advice

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard attempt to distract Sheldon with a trip to a historic railway when he slows the progress of their work on the Air Force guidance system. Raj and Stuart look after baby Halley, while Penny and Amy take Bernadette out

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny offer Sheldon's old room to Raj after he is forced to move out of his apartment to save money, leading to unexpected conflict among the gang and some unwanted observations from Leonard's mother. Christine Baranski guests

programme.title: Night at the Museum: Battle of the Smithsonian (2009)
programme.desc: The living exhibits at New York's Museum of Natural History are shipped to Washington's Smithsonian Institution - along with the artefact that brought them to life in the first place. Nightwatchman Larry tries to retrieve it, only to find the contents of the world's largest museum have been reanimated, including a pharaoh determined to take over the world. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Amy Adams, Owen Wilson and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox USA
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces in the US turn their hand to being the country's most opinionated viewers as they critique the week's TV. Shows watched include Undercover Boss, Floor Is Lava, Jersey Shore: Family Vacation, Dating Around, Selling Sunset, and Taken

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Joe Swash and Stacey Solomon, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Martin Kemp and son Roman Kemp, Maureen Lipman and Gyles Brandreth, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Laura Whitmore and Iain Stirling, Rylan Clark-Neal and Linda Clark, Georgia Taylor and Sunetra Sarker, and Susanna Reid and Rob Rinder offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques. Shows under scrutiny include 127 Hours, Planet Earth II, Cape Fear, This Morning on public toilets, EastEnders, Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad, and BBC news reports on beaches and travel

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Oprah with Meghan and Harry and the subsequent news that Piers Morgan had resigned as co-host of Good Morning Britain. programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Astronaut-in-training Nicky hopes to find romance with well-travelled motoring journalist Daniel. The restaurant also welcomes road sweeper Bruce, whose date is mother of two Yvonne. programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Archaeologist Sophie hopes Second World War enthusiast Mark will be the man for her. Plus, mum-of-four Heather, 72, hopes retired businessman Gary will keep her company in her twilight years

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. As Jack recruits the Grim Reaper and covers Becka's wedding dress in fake blood, he hopes his eerie marital concept will prove a screaming success and not a fright night disaster

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Party fanatic Sonny plans a zoo-themed Ibiza beach party wedding, but his wife-to-be Jazmin wants a sophisticated and timeless wedding

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp plans to surprise her guests with two main courses - roast beef with mulled wine gravy, and masala cauliflower with coconut stuffing for the vegetarians

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leyla and Will Moore from Leeds both have hectic working lives, but now the five kids they have between them are grown up, they're ready to slow down a little and buy their very own place in the sun. With their budget of £350,000 Scarlette Douglas shows them five stunning villas on the Algarve in Portugal

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The duo are in Hay-on-Wye and Bedford, where Henry Cole restores a bespoke workbench and Simon O'Brien ponders the possibilities of some discarded planks of wood

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers are on the rock in Ireland, searching for the beneficiaries to the estate of a Limerick-born man. Another case reveals the compulsive hoarding habits of a car enthusiast, and uncovers a potentially highly valuable collection of Dinky toys. Meanwhile, the work of the team brings about an emotional family reunion

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The owners of a vineyard open a pop-up restaurant, but there are some last-minute nerves, while two B&B proprietors host an unusual event in a bid to boost takings. In Spain, a wannabe fishing captain has to learn the ropes

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Rob and Jo Smith's dog-friendly B&B in Hope Mountain, north Wales. Eyebrows are raised when self-styled 'queens of clean' Steffi Stephenson and Sarah Banks find evidence of their pet hate. During an afternoon making jam, identical twins Fernando and Emilio Tundidor reveal a competitive streak. At breakfast, Denise and Alan Newman are surprisingly demanding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Beaumont Hotel in Louth, Lincolnshire, two guests are damning about the breakfast quality, so it remains to be seen how managers Steffi Stevenson and Sarah Banks will react

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Rock House Hotel in Lynmouth, Devon, where owner Emilio Tundidor is being ably assisted by his identical twin brother Fernando

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Troy House in Painswick, Gloucestershire, where owners Alan and Denise Newman hope the guests will love their rooms, but Emilio and Fernando are on the warpath

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day in the competition sees the B&B owners meet up for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives are on the road in Southampton as they try to track down multiple beneficiaries to the estate of a local market trader worth more than £100,000. Meanwhile, another firm's investigation reveals a family's historical links to the beginnings of professional sport

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Tortoises Burt and Terrance become rivals in love when they pursue a breeding female. Meanwhile, six-year-old okapi K'Tusha nears the end of her first pregnancy, and keepers hope that her baby will help to conserve this highly endangered species, and Asian elephant bull Aung-Bo senses that the three-day window for mating is about to begin

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy.
