Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Simon and Jasmine Dale take on the challenging task of building a 21st-century, low-impact three-bedroom family home, starting the build with only £500 in the bank. As part of a pioneering, government-backed, sustainable village called Lammas, the couple must adhere to their fierce planning conditions of self-sufficiency or risk losing everything

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: Jenny is on the chase to stop a killer as they leave a bloody trail across the city. The stakes are even higher when she realises her son has been in contact with the mass murderer. Canadian crime drama, starring Serinda Swan and Roger Cross

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Glyn, 53, is brought in by ambulance after a work-related accident on a building site, and in his hour of need he turns to his lifelong friend Barrie. Eighteen-month-old Zemirah's parents are concerned with her diarrhoea and vomiting and 93-year-old John is admitted after falling at home, but also has a severe case of sepsis

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson takes on guest captain Johnny Vegas in another round of words-and-numbers chaos. Their team-mates are Alex Brooker and Sara Pascoe, while Claudia Winkleman and Rob Beckett stand in for Rachel Riley on letters-and-numbers duty, and Tom Allen is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. Hosted by Jimmy Carr

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: Jenny is on the chase to stop a killer as they leave a bloody trail across the city. The stakes are even higher when she realises her son has been in contact with the mass murderer. Canadian crime drama, starring Serinda Swan and Roger Cross

programme.title: Secret Bridesmaids' Business
programme.desc: Melanie turns stalker in a bid to save another woman from Jakob's harassment, while Olivia discovers Alex's darkest secret, and Saskia starts to spiral in the aftermath of a vicious attack. Drama, starring Abbie Cornish, Katie McGrath and Georgina Haig

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Glyn, 53, is brought in by ambulance after a work-related accident on a building site, and in his hour of need he turns to his lifelong friend Barrie. Eighteen-month-old Zemirah's parents are concerned with her diarrhoea and vomiting and 93-year-old John is admitted after falling at home, but also has a severe case of sepsis

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of favourite investigations, with Jimmy Doherty heading to South Africa on the trail of sweet piquante peppers and visits the company that has exclusive rights to grow, process and market these colourful chillies. Kate Quilton is in Hawaii to investigate the potentially dangerous levels of mercury in tuna, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why the bags of ice cubes in supermarkets are so crystal clear

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is taught how to bind books by expert Isabelle Ting, and reveals how to create a notebook from leftover materials including leather offcuts, recycled leather coats and decorative paper

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas comes to the aid of brothers Dave and Richard Paine, who are looking for a home in Portugal's Algarve that can serve as a winter retreat for their parents, but could also be used by their families for holidays throughout the rest of the year

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: After the detectives' hard work, a person visits the home of a relative she didn't know existed, and who surprisingly lives just 10 minutes' from her own house. On another case, the hunters experience a strange sense of deja vu when they trace heirs to the estate of a woman they have met before, and their search uncovers the remarkable history of Thames bargemen. Plus, details of more unclaimed estates are divulged

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: As the season draws to a close, a couple hoping to run a fishing charter business face a big decision. Two bed-and-breakfast owners have to cope with some tricky customers, while the first guests book into an observatory business in rural France - but how will the owners go about pleasing the punters?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook in Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling, who's joined by her friend Delia Duell, hopes that her uptempo personality will be a hit with the guests. However, guests Andrea and John are unimpressed to discover that they're in self-catering. At breakfast, Italian cousins Massimo and Raf aren't keen on the home from home approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent, owners Neale and Julie Taylor hope their exclusively vegetarian B&B will stand out from the crowd

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers investigate the case of a famous Blackpool accordion player, and reveal a tragic tale of separation involving two brothers who died on the same day in the same hospital after not seeing each other for many years. Plus, the team explores the history of Rolls-Royce and the Crewe railways, before sharing details of other unclaimed estates

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Visayan warty pig Wendy is fed up with the attentions of male pig Jake and decides to escape. Meanwhile, young giraffe Narus has been battling with other young males in the herd, so the keepers decide the time has come to move him to a new zoo. Which is easier said than done

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie finishes her mother Isobel's apartment. Painted and decorated, it now has her fathers framed pictures on the walls, a stunning 200-year-old mahogany canopy, plus furniture. Elsewhere, Jayne and Steve finish the Oriental Room and are ready to take in guests

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Mark and Candida Diacono, who have set about building a home in the shape of a plough on their 17-acre smallholding in Devon. With its complex curved roof, timber-clad exterior and steel-framed working barn, the ambitious project presents a unique technical challenge for a local builder and his team of employees, and the task proves even more intimidating when considering Mark and Candida hope to complete work on the build within seven months

programme.title: Ice Cold Murders: Rocco Schiavone
programme.desc: The Berguet case is re-opened when the instigator of the kidnapping of Chiara is murdered in prison. Rocco begins investigating the murder, spending whole days in prison interrogating prisoners and prison guards. He's determined to find the top echelon of the criminal organisation which - with the complicity of members of the Aosta Valley bourgeoisie - seems to control the region's economy. Crime thriller, starring Marco Giallini. In Italian

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Danny, who's 41, is brought into hospital after falling 20 feet through a skylight onto concrete. Meanwhile, 27-year-old Tara receives treatment for her severe abdominal pains, and three-year-old Clara is brought to A&E by her mum, with a chickpea stuck up her nose

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Maria, a 52-year-old woman who suffers from MS, is brought in with suspected sepsis. As doctors work to regulate her temperature, her husband Duncan talks about their life together and how they supported each other through her diagnosis. Meanwhile, Eve, who's 83, is in A&E with her husband John after she fell down the stairs into her basement, suffering an open ankle fracture. Elsewhere, six-year-old Grace has come to hospital with her dad Ben after getting a piece of tissue stuck inside her ear

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Sean Lock face off once again in another unpredictable battle of words and numbers, with help from Kevin Bridges and Rebecca Front. Rachel Riley is on maths-and-letters duty, and Joe Lycett is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Danny, who's 41, is brought into hospital after falling 20 feet through a skylight onto concrete. Meanwhile, 27-year-old Tara receives treatment for her severe abdominal pains, and three-year-old Clara is brought to A&E by her mum, with a chickpea stuck up her nose

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of favourite investigations from the team. Jimmy Doherty hears about a threat to British juniper berries that could mark the end of gin's recent resurgence in popularity, while Kate Quilton delves into the contents of herbal bedtime teas to establish if they really can help people get to sleep more easily. Big oyster fan Matt Tebbutt wonders why he never finds pearls in his seafood platters so sets off for China, where he discovers that pearl production is full of surprises

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits a green bean plantation in Kenya to find out how they grow the beans so straight for the supermarkets, and finds out what happens to those that don't make the grade. At a Norwegian scallop farm, Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join the divers harvesting scallops by hand from the seabed, and Kate Quilton tries to unravel a mystery about Cornwall's most famous local delicacy

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Glenda and Tina from Dorset are looking for the perfect holiday home in Portugal's western Algarve. These nature-loving culture vultures are looking for somewhere where they can spend time walking their dog Lady and enjoying the rich local history. Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for their £100,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bev Cameron has multiple sclerosis and finds that a warm climate helps her manage her symptoms. As a response, she has decided to buy a property near the historic harbour town of Paphos, in Cyprus. Enlisting the help of Bev's best friend Shelley, Laura Hamilton delivers five potential homes for her budget of £90,000, in the hopes that one of them will fit the bill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Reality TV star and choreographer Louie Spence and his husband Leto Fernandez have long-dreamed of owning a holiday home in Spain's Almeria Province - somewhere to spend the winter months and short summer breaks with friends and family. Laura Hamilton is on hand to show them five properties within their £185,000 budget, but will any of them have Louie high-kicking with delight?

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Pastry chefs from hotels, restaurants and small businesses in Cardiff, Bristol, Norfolk, Brighton and London compete to be crowned best patisserie team in Britain. The teams face testing challenges judged by two world-class patisserie experts, Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden. For their first challenge, the teams must produce two different types of miniature classics - Tarte Piémontaise and the Jaffa Cake. They must make 24 perfect and identical items of each pastry in just three and a half hours

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Liverpool, where the first to host is 41-year-old taxi business owner Rob, who plans a Scouse themed evening. The guests he is hoping to impress are 23-year-old fitness fanatic Toria, 44-year-old property developer Christine, 40-year-old South African business woman Sam, and 20-year-old student Inzy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is night two in Liverpool, and the turn of businesswoman Sam to try and impress with her South-African-themed evening, though her food is a bit hit and miss

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Gym worker Toria hosts the third party in Liverpool, and hopes a kissing game around the dinner table, along with her flaky flapjacks, will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Undergraduate Inzy is the fourth host in Liverpool, and he is on a mission to prove students really can cook. The flirting between him and Toria continues, but will he allow love to stand in way of the competition?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Liverpool, property developer Christine plans to wow her guests with a Spanish holiday-themed dinner party. However, the mood is rather more feisty than fiesta as on-going tensions finally come to a head and it's not long before the honesty - and the sparks - begin to fly

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, B&B veteran Paul Workman, who runs Hamlet House in Stratford-upon-Avon, makes the most of his town's Shakespeare connection as another round of the competition gets under way - but his guests cause a fuss when a hair is found in the mushrooms at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to B&B novices Ben Gray and Katie Keegan's Ashlack business in Cumbria. The guests admire rooms in the estate's 17th-century manor house, but Veronica Pickles and Saida Hammoud are unimpressed when they discover their accommodation is a yurt in the woods. Filled with dread at the idea of a midnight trip to the toilet facilities, they later make plain their feelings when checking out

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Richwood in Torquay, where partners Veronica Pickles and Saida Hammoud run the 13-bedroom guest house by themselves. The diners are given a crash course in belly-dancing and provided with a feast of Lebanese delicacies - but are soon distracted by the dated decor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Helen Fraser welcomes her guests to the Tai'r Bull Inn in the Brecon Beacons, with moral support from her friend Kath. Ben and Katie are quick to praise the quality of their lodgings, but B&B veteran Paul spots a few mistakes, while Saida and Veronica refuse to spend the night in their room after they make a discovery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. A clash then ensues between two of the week's hosts before the winner of the competition is revealed

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw travel to Devon to steal away one of the fastest road cars of its time, a 1997 Mitsubishi Evo 4, which dominated the World Rally circuits in the 1990s. The car that Fuzz and Tim are taking on belongs to 60-year-old Gary, a petrolhead who loved his Evo and used it as his daily driver

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Cameras follow The Jacobite steam train across the Glenfinnan Viaduct. Vloggers Geoff and Vicki, who have visited all 2,567 of Britain's railway stations, board the Caledonian Sleeper on a 24-hour return journey to the UK's highest station at Corrour. Plus, a team of engineers scramble to erect the UK's largest landslide barrier when a slip threatens a section of the world-famous West Highland Line

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode focuses on the chances people take in life and whether a gamble ever pays off. 20-year-old Megan is rushed to St George's hospital after being involved in a high speed car crash, and as doctors begin treatment and work to assess her injuries, it soon becomes clear how her hedonistic lifestyle has put her at risk. programme.desc: Catie Munnings puts the BAC Mono R through its paces at Goodwood. Duncan Pittaway explores the Bugatti Type 35. Engineer Dan Dickrell discovers swamp track racing in Florida

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a road trip, trying to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East. However, they opt to do so in open-top sports cars that were built for significantly less demanding tasks than driving along the conflict-ridden mountains of southern Turkey, through war-ravaged Iraq and across the searing deserts of Syria. The trio also try to find out what myrrh is and where it can be bought

programme.desc: Detectives Mike Lowrey and Marcus Burnett search for a $100million heroin consignment brazenly stolen by a French drug kingpin from the police evidence room. However, after a mix-up over a phone call from a potential witness, each cop has to assume the other's identity - Lowrey becoming the henpecked family man and Burnett the playboy. Michael Bay's action thriller, starring Will Smith, Martin Lawrence, Tea Leoni, Tcheky Karyo and Theresa Randle

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges hosts the stand-up show from the Hammersmith Apollo in London, warming up the audience before introducing routines by DJ, Buzzcocks regular and all-round funnyman Phill Jupitus and actress-comedienne Sara Pascoe, making her Apollo debut

programme.title: Mock the Week

As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another. From November 2019

programme.title: Brojects in the House
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and Kevin turn traditional home and cottage renovation projects on their head

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Divers explore the wreck of First World War battleship HMS Audacious, which was once the pride of the Royal Navy and thought to be 'unsinkable' until a single unexpected blow sealed its fate 14 miles off the coast of Northern Ireland

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Part one of two. Using the latest diving technology to explore the South-West's deep-sea wrecks

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates theories that Hitler faked his suicide at the end of the Second World War and may have escaped from his Berlin bunker

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length and breadth of the country with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins in Liverpool by learning to speak Scouse, before researching the first railway fatality and exploring the origins of the Eccles cake

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The second leg of Michael Portillo's journey from Liverpool to Scarborough takes him from Manchester to Bury. Along the way, he finds out about the life of Victorian train enthusiast George Bradshaw, gets fitted for a trilby hat in Denton, and learns how railways helped contribute to the nation's love affair with fish and chips

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Helmingham Hall, a Tudor manor house with two working drawbridges in Suffolk. The experts cast their eyes over a medieval sword which was found at the bottom of a golf course pond, an Indian jewel dating back to the battle of Seringapatam in 1799, and a collection of decorative boxes from the 17th and 18th centuries

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.desc: An exploration of the Harz Mountain Railway in Germany - Europe's longest rail network - which still uses steam locomotives and has a murky, hidden past

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A delightful collection of British classics comes up for auction, including an Austin A35 and a Hillman Avenger Estate in stunning condition. An E-Type Jaguar also hits the auction

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An abandoned prison beneath the water, a tunnel in the heart of a mountain, concrete towers hidden in the woods and a rusting coastal relic

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The First World War led to a number of astounding war factories, which laid the foundations and paved the way for modern factories of today

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Some of the most shocking cases connected with the regime's bloody reign, beginning with the case of Hitler's niece Geli Raubal, who was found dead in his apartment in 1931

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Helmingham Hall, a Tudor manor house with two working drawbridges in Suffolk. The experts cast their eyes over a medieval sword which was found at the bottom of a golf course pond, an Indian jewel dating back to the battle of Seringapatam in 1799, and a collection of decorative boxes from the 17th and 18th centuries

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey, travelling on the Embsay and Bolton Abbey steam railway, finding out about Roman discoveries in York, and taking to the air in the Network Rail helicopter

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey inspired by George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook. He searches for the last liquorice grower in Pontefract, finds out how the railways transformed Hull into one of the largest white-fish ports in the world, and goes fishing for sea bass in Bridlington

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Moskovitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a loving home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: With the Blitz taking its toll, Britain had to keep its manufacturing going. Shadow factories were born and were crucial in the production of ammunition

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How in devising the F35 Lightnin II, engineers have pushed the boundaries of aviation technology further than ever before and created a brand new, revolutionary, fighter aircraft capable of handling threats in the air and on the ground. programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Autobahn
programme.desc: Lorry driver Jorg transports parts of a giant crawler crane. Conservationist Joe relocates some cows, and two paramedics help the victims of an accident.

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: The Indian Pacific is a massive locomotive that journeys across Australia. How does it traverse some of the most scenic and remote areas a train has ever gone?

programme.title: Mega Shippers
programme.desc: The pressure is on to export three offshore wind towers to safeguard a Scottish town's economic future. Plus, a 130-ton locomotive is winched onto a narrow trailer

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Orange County Choppers team designs a custom motorbike for an outdoor lifestyle company, and are inspired to test their survival skills in the great outdoors

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: French polisher Alex is excited to work on a 100-year-old table. And, paper conservator Ashely has to take safety precautions to work on a mouldy poster.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: A look at life on board the Marina, Oceania Cruises' largest ship, which makes a 12-day, five port voyage. Its mobility lets it glide through narrow channels, steer into tight ports and carry 1,200 passengers

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of mini GP motorcycles, fig biscuits, toolboxes and pipe bends

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of replica revolvers, used-oil furnaces, vegetable peelers and pizza cutters

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Preston Hotel in Yeovil, owned by father and son Bis and Deep Oozageer. Ann and Rebecca find their room more cheap than cheerful, although an afternoon tasting the produce at a cider brandy distillery proves a massive hit

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples compete in Plymouth. The first hosts, Mena and her husband Chris, put together a locally sourced menu, and a boogie on the piano impresses their guests. Lovebirds Vicky and her boyfriend Chris host next, requesting everyone to come dressed as a famous couple. The final hosts are seafaring married couple Sarah and Chris, whose guests make a gallant effort with their costumes for a nautical evening

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Ginny and Tony, who are searching for a property on the Mani Peninsula in Greece and have a budget of £230,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Peter and Julia Andrews from Hull love Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands and want to buy a holiday home there, which their whole family can enjoy. Armed with a budget of £60,000, Sara Damergi scours the island for five perfect lock-up-and-leave properties, but will any of them make the grade?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Dean and Karen Dhinsa find a rural retreat in southern Tenerife. The pair dream of spending their retirement in a sunny climate, and are looking for a home within easy reach of the island's beaches, complete with a cave to transform into a cinema, all on a budget of £180,000

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business manager Ross Chapman hosts the first dinner party in west Lancashire, but encounters a string of culinary disasters while preparing his fish-themed menu. However, he still finds time for a manicure and bubble bath before the meal, and it is the behaviour of one of his guests, rather than his burnt tart, that causes the most embarrassment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Single mother Lindsay Pulford hosts the second party in west Lancashire, hoping to impress her fellow diners with a Caribbean-themed night. However, as soon as the guests arrive, they begin to regale her with reminders of her antics the previous evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History student and part-time bartender Chris Mosey hosts the third dinner party in west Lancashire. He is confident about his cooking skills and has designed a menu that includes blue cheesecake and Scouse tapas

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cleaner Louise Dee hosts the penultimate dinner party in west Lancashire and provides her guests with a Spanish-inspired menu and an 'old skool rave' before her rivals give their thoughts on the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Medical photographer Lorraine Rimmer hosts the final dinner party in west Lancashire, and hopes to impress her guests with a menu consisting of authentic Spanish cuisine, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge return to help more British people making French castles their home or running businesses from them. Clive and Karen and their two children are preparing for the wedding season at their chateau in the Dordogne Valley, mucking in together to renovate the pool house

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After enjoying many holidays together on Tenerife, Terry and Jen wish to celebrate their recent engagement by buying their own holiday home on the south of the island. To help them fulfil their dream, Jonnie Irwin hunts out five options for their £150,000 budget for the pair to consider

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: After two gruelling years during which Johnny Vegas has seen his plans for his glampsite hit by every problem imaginable, he is finally ready for the grand unveiling. Unfortunately, it's in front of his harshest critics - his family and friends. With the final vehicles arriving, there's time for Johnny to deceive his assistant Bev one more time, before he presents her with the 1950s camper van he bought and restored as a gift. As last-minute preparations on the site continue, Johnny inspects the renovated fire truck which quickly takes a wrong turn as he manages to get stuck on the roof, and things get worse from there. As he and Bev prepare for the arrival of the much-loved Maltese bus that started the whole adventure, disaster strikes one final time

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with three house-hunters with two very different needs, but one shared goal. In 2018, schoolteacher Katy, her comedian husband Charlie, baby daughter Lola, and pet pooch Harper were on the hunt for their first family home in Brighton. Having moved in with Katy's mum to save for a deposit, they had not only grown used to home comforts, but also the vibrant village atmosphere of pricey Fiveways - an area where their property dreams didn't match their budget. With Phil's help they widened the net, viewing four fantastic finds in and around Brighton. Two years later, Phil's back to see whether life on the coast has been plain sailing. And in 2019, Kirstie was scouring east London with 'granny on the go' Janet. Having been diagnosed with a degenerative back condition, Janet was desperate to ditch her lift-less, third storey flat in exchange for a ground-floor forever home. The tricky east London property market was proving tough to navigate though, and Kirstie soon f

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: Sicario 2: Soldado (2018)
programme.desc: The drug war on the US-Mexico border has escalated as the cartels have begun trafficking terrorists across the US border. Federal agent Matt Graver re-teams with operative Alejandro Gillick to fight the enemy forces. Thriller sequel, starring Josh Brolin, Benicio Del Toro and Isabela Moner

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A look back at the times when what started as great chemistry in the studio ended in dating disaster, reliving memorable arguments and cringeworthy moments, including bisexual Leah from the third series, who brought her pet dog Kush to the studio to help her pick her partner. There is also a reunion between Cathy from Hertfordshire and her date Andy

programme.title: Catching Paedophiles: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: Film exploring the challenges of investigating and prosecuting child sexual abuse, and how to manage predators when they've been caught. Detective Claire Lyons, who works for Hampshire Constabulary's Internet Child Abuse Team, has to prove that her suspicions are right about a man who has been uploading indecent images of children - and not risk any mistakes that would lead to the suspect walking free and being able to offend again

programme.title: The Year Britain Stopped
programme.desc: Documentary examining personal stories of Britain during lockdown. The programme includes an interview with the nurse who treated Boris Johnson in intensive care, and examines the news reports written in the event of his death. Other stories include the scientists and frontline workers striving to control the crisis, and a single mother whose son went missing during the first lockdown

programme.title: 21 Day Body Turnaround with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist and doctor asks five volunteers to put their lives in his hands as he attempts to turn their health around in just 21 days. Initial testing reveals all of them to likely be at risk from debilitating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, heart disease, chronic inflammation and stress. Most have the aerobic fitness age of someone at least 20 years older. In the first episode, Michael investigates their digestive health and starts them out on a new training plan in conjunction with GP and fitness trainer Dr Folusha Oluwajana

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners reunite, ready to air their grievances from throughout the week. Ann and Rebecca from Grange Farm Park aren't happy with Marilyn marking them down for the lack of a hot tub, and Marilyn herself isn't too pleased when her and Neil review the feedback for their slow breakfast service at Edgecliff Boutique B&B

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Plymouth, three couples compete to host the best dinner party and win the £1000 cash prize. Sales assistant Michelle and supermarket manager Shaun's classical evening gets the week off to a stylish start, although their guests' interpretation of classical is a little different. Next up, charity worker Amber and marine engineer Lee's basic Mexican menu doesn't go down very well. On the last night, retired businessmen Adrian and Mark's well thought-out menu and unusual entertainment bring everything to a lively conclusion

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps recently retired Clover and Mark, who have decided after 30 years of living in Manchester to find a new home and start a new life in Axarquía, Andalucía

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman heads to northern Tenerife to help Edinburgh couple John Clark and David Cameron find a dwelling that will eventually become their retirement home. With their wedding just weeks away, the pair have £100,000 to find somewhere that will accommodate a future mother-in-law as well as five visiting children

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Liz and Grahame Pink, who have saved hard for their dream holiday home in Lanzarote and have a budget of £63,000 to spend on a property

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: National Heritage worker Gillian Sutherland hosts the first dinner party in Inverness, and has high hopes that her guests - emergency-call handler Melissa Kirby, property tycoon David Edes, hair stylist Zarina Ainslie and personal fitness trainer Jamie Tulloch - will appreciate her traditional Scottish menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Designer-label fan Melissa Kirby hosts the second dinner party in Inverness, hoping her cooking lessons from one of the city's best chefs will win her the £1,000 prize. However, rival Gillian is unimpressed when she finds out the chef also made the gravy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Property tycoon and teetotaller David Edes hosts a nautical evening for the third dinner party in Inverness, where his menu of 'basic but wholesome food' makes his guests wary. A night of suspicion and hilarity between the diners ensues

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the contest in Inverness, it is hairdresser Zarina Ainslie's turn to host. The fun-loving hopeful needs to live up to her lofty claims of being an amazing cook as her fellow contestants prepare to rate her on the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional footballer Jamie Tulloch hosts the final party in Inverness, going to the trouble of ensuring all the ingredients in his chosen dishes are locally sourced. However, it remains to be seen whether his home-grown approach to fine dining will be enough to impress his discerning fellow competitors, and as the night draws to a close, the week's winner is revealed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Clive, Karen, Ross and Abbie prepare for the first wedding of the season, Tim and Krys remove the render from their barn, and Tim and Margreeth start creating a potager garden

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: North Wales couple Rebecca and Carl want to find their very own bolthole in Gandia, Valencia, that will start as a holiday home but one day become a permanent home. With their children now grown up, they feel now is the right time to make the move. Ideally, they want enough land to grow their own fruit and veg and to be near the beach, so Rebecca can collect shells and stones that inspire her craft jewellery business. Armed with a budget of £80,000, Jean Johansson shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets Devon woman Tabitha, who is using a horsebox to build a mobile juice bar for the second time after her first one was stolen. He also continues his tour of New Zealand's amazing spaces, and he and Will Hardie's futuristic rotating home takes a giant leap forward

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Farmers Ali and Rich reveal how they hope to salvage one of the rarest caravans in the world, complete with a spiral staircase and roof garden, and Devon fireman Nick plans to transform an old doghouse into a studio garden apartment for his mother-in-law. The presenter visits a fold-out beach hut in New Zealand, and an electric wheelchair provides the engineering breakthrough George and Will need to rotate their futuristic home

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with three house-hunters with two very different needs, but one shared goal. programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Natalka Greenwood and Sam Watson treat a 10-year-old autistic boy who has injured his back on a trampoline. Emma Snelson and Lizzie Hobday rush to a baby who has stopped breathing

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A look back at the times when what started as great chemistry in the studio ended in dating disaster, reliving memorable arguments and cringeworthy moments, including bisexual Leah from the third series, who brought her pet dog Kush to the studio to help her pick her partner. There is also a reunion between Cathy from Hertfordshire and her date Andy

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner: 10 Years and a Lovely Bit of Squirrel
programme.desc: A celebration of Robert Popper's iconic comedy Friday Night Dinner, to mark its 10-year anniversary. The sitcom, which coined the catchphrase 'lovely bit of squirrel', centred on the fortunes of the eccentric Goodman family and their even more surreal neighbour Jim. With behind-the-scenes material and never-before-seen outtakes, this documentary features interviews with stars including Tamsin Greig, Paul Ritter, Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Mark Heap and Tracy-Ann Oberman. It also includes the thoughts of celebrity fans Claudia Winkleman, Joe Thomas, David Baddiel and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The recruits are tested on how well they can control their emotions by three tests, including a backward dive at night, an illusion jump and a challenge designed to push their ability to keep their aggression in check. A shock is in store when they discover the identity of an undercover staff member in their ranks

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Pastry chefs from hotels, restaurants and small businesses in Cardiff, Bristol, Norfolk, Brighton and London compete to be crowned best patisserie team in Britain. The teams face testing challenges judged by two world-class patisserie experts, Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden. For their first challenge, the teams must produce two different types of miniature classics - Tarte Piémontaise and the Jaffa Cake. They must make 24 perfect and identical items of each pastry in just three and a half hours

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Married couple Rachel and Matt kick off the competition in Nottinghamshire with a hearty menu. Fun-loving Mark makes a big first impression, and his wife Debbie is the butt of most of his jokes, while final couple Sam and Paula are taken aback, but it remains to be seen who will win the £1,000 after all players have delivered their dishes

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to The Waverley Hotel in Callander in Scotland, owned by husband and wife Gordon and Jan Halladay. Ollie and Liz discover that the bathroom bin hasn't been emptied and best friends Tanya and Vicky get the gloves out for a thorough examination. Later, Jan challenges the group to become shipmates for the day and Tanya takes a shine to Liz. Over dinner, the group realise that Jan is the boss in her marriage. The next day the guests are looking forward to finally meeting Gordon, who's cooking breakfast. However, his confidence slips away when he serves over-cooked poached eggs, and Jan and Gordon are shocked and surprised by some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Greenview Guesthouse in Cumbria, owned by Tanya Briggs, with help from best friend Vicky Hope

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Hotel Sheraton in Blackpool, where the group are shocked by the sheer size of the hotel, run by mother and son Liz and Ollie Brown

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Katharine and Rory offer some proper Yorkshire hospitality at Chapter Two Accommodation in Malton, including an afternoon of sausage-making and a belly-busting breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Tanya defends the lack of breakfast offering at Greenview Guesthouse and is left flabbergasted when the payments are revealed

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Ben Hillman shows Birmingham couple Stephen Topp and Christine Barratt five properties for their budget of £100,000 in Davenport, Florida. The pair love theme parks, so would love to find somewhere in close proximity to one

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: David and Megan Penny from Gloucestershire search for a holiday home in the Axarquia area of southern Spain, on a budget of £100,000. Presented by Laura Hamilton

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Linda and Alan Keir, who want to buy a holiday home in the Costa del Sol to escape the climate of the Scottish Highlands. They look at five properties on their budget of £150,000

programme.title: Tom Allen's Quizness
programme.desc: Contestants' general knowledge is put to the test as they play for the chance to win £25,000. Here, Fiona, George, Rosa and Paddy fight it out for a shot at the jackpot, facing tricky questions on anything from egg fried rice to Katie Price

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary featuring one of the most emotional and heart-rending stories ever filmed - that of Noel Fitzpatrick's own dog, Keira. In September 2020, Noel's life was turned upside down when the 13-year-old border terrier was hit by a van right outside the practice. Having sustained a ruptured abdominal lining and urinary bladder, a crushed sacrum and pelvis and a dislocated hip, Noel's colleagues worked tirelessly to repair extensive internal damage. Suddenly, the surgeon renowned for finding solutions for even the most challenging injuries, had to steel himself for the most important surgery of his life

programme.title: Rich Kids, Skint Holiday
programme.desc: Privileged 19-year-old Harry has no experience with children, but manages to build a bond with eight-year-old Connor and the Clifford family on a caravan park in the New Forest. Mum Kate challenges Harry to plan a day's activities for £20

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Mandy and Christian's Pride parade finally arrives, but what begins as a stressful day only gets worse when the generator powering the speakers packs up. Can the duo turn things around or will the the revellers be expected to dance to a silent soundtrack? Meanwhile, cockney limo driver Johnny Diamond books two big jobs on the same night, and Bury couple Andy and Sue settle in to their static caravan in the Spanish countryside

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Sunday's services grind to a halt when the 11.57 service to Penzance derails as it is departing, while a knife-carrying passenger causes concern in Bristol. Elsewhere, a team of specialists clean the mats laid down to protect the tracks from the actions of passengers ignoring the signs advising them not to flush onboard toilets while the trains are in the station

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Some of Britain's filthiest and most cluttered homes are tackled in the space of just three days

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Highlights of the documentary giving an insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads as they combine modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice. Officers pursue a drug runner down one of the UK's busiest motorways, and a drug deal results in a white-knuckle race through Bradford's back streets

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A high-speed night-time pursuit in Bradford heightens tensions in the local community, with outnumbered cops facing the prospect of a potential riot. An apparently routine stop takes a serious turn as the vehicle pulled over is thought to contain a firearm and the unarmed officers must hold their nerve as they wait for a specialist team to join them. Jamie Theakston narrates

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police interception unit attempt to stop a dangerous driver, resulting in multiple cars and a helicopter being called into action. Dog handler Duncan assists armed response officers in Leeds city centre in the early hours following reports of a man carrying a pistol, while in Wakefield, a repeat offender who has been caught drink driving in the past thinks he knows how to beat the system

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dan and Tom have their hands full with a driver who is four times over the limit and still refuses to come quietly even when he's bundled into the back of the police car. Sophie and Adie stop a carload of lads at five in the morning and the driver is over the limit, but it's what's down his trousers that is more of a concern

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of burglars breaks into a house and takes the keys to luxury cars. After the thieves flee in three stolen vehicles, multiple teams hunt them down with the police chopper. The first burglar is caught after a lengthy pursuit of a stolen Mercedes, which he abandons before running straight into the path of waiting officers. Once the occupants of a second stolen Merc are apprehended by the chopper team, Ben and Warren go after the third vehicle - which they are stunned to witness burst into flames

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Master dog trainer Graeme Hall finds long lasting solutions for dogs with problems. They include Butty, who is bullying the female members of his household, and Pixie, who howls like a banshee the moment she sees another dog. Cameras also follow Lucy, who will stay in the park for hours to raid picnics

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Jane returns from Mildura to find tension between Chloe, Nicolette, Aaron and David. Paul grows increasingly suspicious of Harlow's behaviour

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy deflects Tane's question about why she broke up with Dean. A withdrawn Jasmine is out in public for the first time in days

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits by the Sea
programme.desc: In Scarborough, there's a disaster for Danny and boss Rudy at their interactive horror house on the front when the machine that powers the scares breaks down, while Ann reveals the secrets of selling souvenirs in Blackpool. Skegness B&B owners Lorraine and Neville decide whether to sell their business and enjoy retirement

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: One of the department's new junior doctors treats a man with a suspected case of sepsis, that has been triggered by the tracheostomy tube he relies on to breathe. An 84-year-old woman is brought in by ambulance after falling down a flight of stairs and undergoes multiple x-rays to check for broken bones, while a 12-month-old baby needs treatment after having a fit

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A man with a torn aorta, the main artery in the body, needs urgent medical attention to prevent catastrophic internal bleeding. Meanwhile, a patient is flown into the hospital by helicopter after being involved in a major car accident, and another man is brought in and treated after falling through a skylight in a roof

programme.title: Inferno (2016)
programme.desc: Following his life-or-death missions in The Da Vinci Code, and Angels and Demons, long suffering expert Robert Langdon awakes with amnesia in an Italian hospital, and is soon on the run with a beautiful doctor. Racing to stop a devastating virus, the symbologist suffers shocking visions as an assassin closes in. Director Ron Howard's conversion of the Dan Brown thriller, starring Tom Hanks, Felicity Jones and Irrfan Khan

The first edition follows drag duo John and Barry, known as 'Dragtastic', who get to work repairing dozens of outfits, while also dealing with their respective day jobs of cooking and teaching. Elsewhere, pensioners Ray and Christine try to adapt to the high summer heat, while ARCH horse charity founder Jill races to the rescue of a neglected white stallion

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Mobile operations manager Jane is called to Burnham, Buckinghamshire, where a van has struck a bridge, and it is up to her to assess whether any significant damage has been caused to the railway line that runs over it. When a train breaks down on platform 15 of Reading station, staff are under pressure to identify and rectify the issue as quickly as possible to avoid any widespread delays, but fixing a fault with the brakes proves to be very complex

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Richard and Brennan tackle a house in Neath, south Wales, where single father Barry has let his home become increasingly dirty and disorganised. Barry used to lead an active life, but at the age of 32, Barry suffered a stroke, and now cleaning has become an impossible job, so the pair quickly get to work, and set about scrubbing, shining and sorting to make Barry's home a fit and safe place for him and his daughter

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A high-speed chase in Bradford is ended when CS gas is used to bring a determined speeder to justice, while teams are out on quiet back roads as they track two cars that are thought to be connected with various firearms offences. Plus, burglars find out they cannot outrun the cops when weighed down with a quad bike and trailing a cloud of cannabis behind them

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A high-speed chase in Bradford's crowded streets ends very suddenly and the driver claims he just wants to get home to eat his takeaway while it is still hot. But is that the whole story? In Harrogate, a volatile drink-driver's rapid mood swings prove a test for the cops, while a large stash of cannabis is found at the scene of a major collision, which has left one person with life-threatening injuries. A couple suspected of dealing drugs are chased down and taken to the station, where they provide the traffic team with contrasting reactions to being held in custody. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Duncan and the firearms team find themselves in a tense stand-off with suspected drug dealers who could be armed, with German shepherd Tia sent in to search and discovering more than the unit bargained for. Steve has to deal with two teens, one who has crashed a stolen car, and the other who has been involved in a bar brawl, while Kev is poised to take out a car with a stinger

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers go in pursuit of a driver who is so drunk that he can't even keep his car on the road and the chase ends with shocking results. Elsewhere, dog handler Tony encounters a naked man stranded at the side of the road, and Sophie is forced to buy a treat for the entire team due to a rookie mistake

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers track down a high-powered Audi speeding through the back streets of Bradford, which comes to a sudden halt as it crashes into a parked car. In Wakefield, dog handler Duncan and police dog Tia are in hot pursuit of a renegade van, while officers Nick and Bobo rush to a scene of total carnage after a drunk driver has crashed into a house

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Trainer Graeme Hall comes to the aid of more dog owners at their wits' end dealing with their four-legged friends. He helps a woman teach a determined Doberman to obey not just her but her elderly parents as well, reunites two dachshunds who have gone from the best of friends to the worst of enemies, and tries to calm the ardour of a hairdresser's French bulldog

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents set out to find the stolen body of a Civil War general, but Dr Elaine Burns is shocked to discover the entombed corpse of a Marine sergeant instead, eventually reaching the conclusion he was buried alive. Meanwhile, Mossad agent Ziva David returns as a replacement for Kate, much to Gibbs's displeasure. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine who appears to have been leading a double life is shot dead. As the investigation continues, however, the team uncovers a case of identity theft - together with a tangled motive involving a mysterious package and a wronged wife. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a struggling actress in what appears to have been a bungled robbery. When a couple produce a video tape showing the thieves at work, the detectives waste little time in tracking down their suspects. Legal drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Two thieves discover the skeletal remains of a woman who was believed to have died in the September 11 attacks on the World Trade Centre. It transpires that a valuable and unusual ring has been stolen from the body, and the team suspects foul play. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Elisabeth Rohm and Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy tries to prosecute a pilot accused of murdering her married lover for trying to end their relationship, but finds himself battling the Navy and the office of the Judge Advocate General, who seem intent on obstructing his efforts. Acclaimed American detective drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a luxury sports car dealer and identify a link between the victim's last customer and a recent spate of robberies. However, the discovery that their prime suspect has learning difficulties soon leads the team to his manipulative girlfriend. Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: New York's district attorneys investigate the mysterious death of a stockbroker who was dating the daughter of a ruthless cosmetics mogul - and find themselves caught up in a wrangle over whether HRT withdrawal can be used as a defence in law. Police drama, guest starring Lucie Arnaz, with Jerry Orbach and Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy tries to trap a crooked lawyer who bribes his clients - but as he tries to track down the man's accomplice, he realises the investigation has placed his own job on the line. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a marine's wife is shown live on the internet and inquiries point to two housewives who were running their own pornographic website, but have since vanished. The search for the missing pair begins with the webster administrator - and ends with a surprising discovery

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A six-year-old boy begs the team to find his father who, he claims, has been kidnapped. The agents learn the missing man is a lieutenant commander working on a confidential project, but their inquiries lead them to believe he disobeyed orders and deleted the information he was sent to acquire

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of two assassins are delivered to NCIS from overseas - so Tony and Ziva assume their identities and keep a rendezvous at a luxury hotel in a bid to discover who they had been sent to kill. Unfortunately, Gibbs finds out his team is not the only one working on the case. Drama, with Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson helps the victims of drugging and assault separate their hallucinations from their memories, as Rollins hunts for the suspect

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: An officer's teenage stepdaughter is found dead in her car after a night of partying, leading Goren and Eames to investigate her boss - who is also an ex-boyfriend - and his supermodel wife. However, when another supposed lover comes forward, their attention turns to a different source and they must combat a small-town police force trying to protect one of its own. Guest starring Brooke Shields

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan and Wheeler are forced to explain their actions after the stabbing of a fireman leads to a massive brawl and an official inquiry. When questions arise concerning the victim's sexual preference, the detectives link the crime to a similar murder 12 years earlier - but a secret the wife has been keeping could hold the key to solving the case. Drama, starring Chris Noth and Julianne Nicholson

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A bridesmaid is found dead in a cheap motel room, and inquiries lead Goren and Eames to several suspects, including two men she met in a bar. They also question a cabbie who not only admits he picked up the victim shortly before she was murdered, but also proves to be a method actor in traning for his next role - as a serial killer

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Inquiries into the death of two sisters lead the team to a voodoo-practising nurse - who does not take kindly to being investigated and responds by placing a curse on Logan. Adding to the strange atmosphere surrounding the case, one of the chief witnesses later drops dead in the street. Chris Noth and Annabella Sciorra star

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Jason represents a teenager arrested for robbing a jewellery store with an older man, arguing that she was a victim of his abuse and not his willing accomplice

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Jason takes a psychologist friend as a client when the therapist is sued for malpractice following a patient's deadly rampage. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are assigned to investigate the execution-style killing of a prominent businessman, and discover his wife was having an affair. However, further evidence emerges, suggesting a different motive - and an unlikely conspirator. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green look into the shooting of defence lawyer Vance Grodie, who had just won an acquittal for a suspected cop killer. Their investigations reveal a link to a network of conservative extremists, and the discovery leads the case into an examination of civil rights. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate a seemingly random drive-by shooting, but soon suspect the victim's husband may know more than he is letting on. To make matters worse, the hospital in which the victim was pronounced dead appears to have contributed to her demise - putting a highly respected surgeon's reputation on the line. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A police officer is shot dead and the chief suspect's confession appears to bring an end to the matter, but his media-savvy lawyer presents a case accusing the force of a string of errors - as well as perceived racism. Guest starring Gregory Hines, in his final acting role before his death

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the fatal stabbing of a taxi driver whose body was left under his vehicle. The finger of suspicion points to a man who took a drunken ride in the cab on the same night - an ex-convict who is also a gifted writer - and his protege, whose fingerprints were found on a book and cigar left at the scene. Saul Rubinek, JK Simmons and Sonia Braga guest star alongside Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An answering machine message leads Briscoe and Curtis to reopen an investigation into an arson attack that killed a girl - but the 15-year-old suspect has a powerful ally in his grandfather, an influential force in political spheres. Award-winning police drama, guest starring Robert Vaughn (Hustle) alongside Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Fingerprint evidence implicates Tony in a woman's death and he finds himself accused of murder. Convinced of his innocence, the other members of the team set out to clear him - even though all the facts seem to indicate he is guilty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An undercover police office is killed by McGee while on duty, and Jenny approves an investigation into the death. But the decision places a strain on her relationship with Gibbs as the team tries to prove that the agent was not culpable, despite the evidence building against him. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A model dies while filming a reality TV show at a US Marine base, and an autopsy reveals she was killed by a drug overdose administered by someone else. Inquiries lead the team to identify three possible suspects - but one of them is also murdered. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Torres relies on his team's investigative skills after he awakens on a dilapidated fishing boat shirtless, disoriented and nursing a pounding headache. His discarded shirt is spattered with blood he is unable to remember any events of the previous 12 hours. Later, he finds the body of a young woman under his motorcycle. Wilmer Valderrama stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the poisoning of a well-known historian, pursuing a line of inquiry that leads to the victim's cash-strapped brother. Unfortunately, he too is later found murdered, and the detectives turn their attention to the two men's wives - only to discover that both stood to gain from being widowed. Drama, guest starring Rip Torn

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames travel to Vietnam to pick up a man who has recently confessed to murdering a 10-year-old actor in 1992. However, it is not long before the detectives find holes in his story, and begin to reassess the original investigation - leading them to believe the suspect may be much closer to home than anyone thought. Guest starring Liza Minnelli

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the death of an education adviser whose body has been found in the East River - a case apparently connected to a series of substantial gambling losses he had run up but was hiding from his wife. At first it appears to have been a suicide, but when it is revealed his body was covered in anti-seasickness patches to induce drowsiness, it becomes clear it was murder

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan investigates a series of murders, following a trail of clues that leads to a local foster mother - but accidentally shoots a fellow cop while pursuing a suspect. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A spate of murder and mayhem shocks Cabot Cove - and it coincides with the arrival of one of Seth's old army friends, who immediately becomes the prime suspect. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica devises an ingenious new board game with a murderous theme - and it is not long before life starts imitating art in deadly fashion. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The owner of a Wild West show is murdered in New Mexico, so Jessica sets out to investigate, helped by a Navajo sheriff with excellent tracking skills. Starring Angela Lansbury, with Graham Greene, Steve Forrest and Mariette Hartley

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: MI6 agent Michael Haggerty is found standing over the body of his estranged daughter's fiance - but Jessica believes there is more to the case than meets the eye and sets out to clear the name of her old friend. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury and Len Cariou

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A psychic predicts the death of a missing tycoon and the location of his body, but the corpse that is found is not that of the businessman. Murder mystery, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

Angela Lansbury, Ron Masak and Sean O'Bryan star

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: One of Jessica's students is wrongly arrested for murder, prompting her to set out to find the real killer and clear the innocent man's name. Guest starring Robert Beltran

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: Kai Winn experiences a vision that drives her to seek aid from a disguised Dukat, and Captain Sisko's relationship with Kasidy is jeopardised. Sci-fi adventure, starring Louise Fletcher and Avery Brooks

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: Part two of two. Having used the Defiant to repel a Tholian attack, the power-hungry Archer commandeers the advanced starship in an attempt to seize control of Earth's entire Terran empire. programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong

programme.desc: A single mother raising three boys begins a new relationship with a charismatic stranger who protects her family from her abusive ex-partner. Fact-based drama, starring Lucas Pittaway, Daniel Henshall and Louise Harris

programme.title: The Dales

Tom Orde-Powlett sets up a boar park at Bolton Castle in Wensleydale, and Adrian Edmondson meets the Leyburn Band during preparations for the Hardraw Scar Brass Band Contest

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: The Leyburn Band takes part in the Hardraw Scar Brass Band Contest, while Amanda Owen and her husband Clive renovate a shepherd's hut on their land. Plus, Adrian Edmondson meets the new landlords of the area's first community-run pub, the George and Dragon in Hudswell, Richmondshire

programme.title: Untamed Romania
programme.desc: In Romania, ancient forests and expansive wetlands provide habitats to some of Europe's most iconic creatures. This is a celebration of nature, in all its beauty and diversity

programme.desc: A schoolboy in post-war London stumbles on a criminal conspiracy whose members use his favourite comic strip to secretly send information to each other about their illegal activities. The police do not take him seriously, so he enlists his friends to help stop the crooks. Ealing comedy, starring Alastair Sim, Harry Fowler, Jack Warner and Valerie White

programme.desc: A lowly TV studio employee sneaks a plug for a friend's product into a show, but when sales rise, everyone wants his publicity services. Comedy, starring Arthur Askey and Sid James

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: A look at individuals who played their part in creating a more culturally diverse post-war Britain, including pioneering actor Earl Cameron, who broke through the colour bar in the film industry. Other well-known names also considered are gay rights activist George Montague, and Brian Rix, who has campaigned passionately on the issue of disability. Plus, Arctic Convoy survivor Austin Byrne and former prisoner of war Jim Purcell share their stories and memories of the era

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Sail the Saronic and Ionian Greek islands with Peter Maneas as he explores their rich history, culture, cuisine, and people as well as their hidden secrets and surprises

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Tom discovers he would qualify for a lump sum from his pension if he were married, so tries to strike up a deal with Sally. Meanwhile, Amber decides to impress her new boss by taking up cycling and, much to Tom's annoyance, receives tuition from Roy on how to ride a bike. Comedy, starring Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Jane Asher

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick's preaching starts to annoy the neighbours and Dirk Bogarde reveals some information to the police. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: The Bromley Boys (2018)
programme.desc: In 1969, with England preparing for the defence of their World Cup crown, 15-year-old David Roberts falls in love with his local non-league club, Bromley FC. While trying to enter the inner sanctum of the club's most loyal fans, David embarks on a seemingly sham relationship with the chairman's daughter, Ruby. Comedy, starring Brenock O'Connor and Jamie Foreman

programme.title: Another Life (2001)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama loosely based on the well-publicised murder of Percy Thompson during the 1920s. Starring Natasha Little, Nick Moran, Ioan Gruffudd and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: The Best of IBA Fight Night
programme.desc: Alan Mortlock presents the best bouts from his IBA unlicensed boxing events from across London and Essex
